options.C: Use the Amazon SageMaker Model Registry to create a model group for models hosted in Amazon ECR. Create a new AWS account. In the new account, use the SageMaker Model Registry as the central catalog. Attach a cross-account resource policy to each model group in the initial AWS accounts.
options.D: Use an AWS Glue Data Catalog to store the models. Run an AWS Glue crawler to migrate the models from the ECR repositories to the Data Catalog. Configure cross-account access to the Data Catalog.

explanation: SageMaker Model Registry in a central governance account with cross-account resource policies provides a unified model catalog without duplicating container images across accounts.

question: A company has developed a new ML model. The company requires online model validation on 10% of the traffic before the company fully releases the model in production. The company uses an Amazon SageMaker endpoint behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB) to serve the model. Which solution will set up the required online validation with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Use production variants to add the new model to the existing SageMaker endpoint. Set the variant weight to 0.1 for the new model. Monitor the number of invocations by using Amazon CloudWatch.
options.B: Use production variants to add the new model to the existing SageMaker endpoint. Set the variant weight to 1 for the new model. Monitor the number of invocations by using Amazon CloudWatch.
options.C: Create a new SageMaker endpoint. Use production variants to add the new model to the new endpoint. Monitor the number of invocations by using Amazon CloudWatch.
options.D: Configure the ALB to route 10% of the traffic to the new model at the existing SageMaker endpoint. Monitor the number of invocations by using AWS CloudTrail.

explanation: A/B testing with production variants allocates a percentage of traffic (e.g., 10%) to a new model variant, enabling live comparison of model performance on real users.

question: A company needs to develop an ML model. The model must identify an item in an image and must provide the location of the item. Which Amazon SageMaker algorithm will meet these requirements?

options.A: Image classification
options.B: XGBoost
options.C: Object detection
options.D: K-nearest neighbors (k-NN)

explanation: SageMaker built-in Object Detection (SSD/YOLO-based) identifies objects in images and returns bounding boxes with class labels and confidence scores — ideal for localization tasks.

question: A company has an Amazon S3 bucket that contains 1 TB of files from different sources. The S3 bucket contains the following file types in the same S3 folder: CSV, JSON, XLSX, and Apache Parquet. An ML engineer must implement a solution that uses AWS Glue DataBrew to process the data. The ML engineer also must store the final output in Amazon S3 so that AWS Glue can consume the output in the future. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use DataBrew to process the existing S3 folder. Store the output in Apache Parquet format.
options.B: Use DataBrew to process the existing S3 folder. Store the output in AWS Glue Parquet format.
options.C: Separate the data into a different folder for each file type. Use DataBrew to process each folder individually. Store the output in Apache Parquet format.
options.D: Separate the data into a different folder for each file type. Use DataBrew to process each folder individually. Store the output in AWS Glue Parquet format.

explanation: AWS Glue crawlers automatically discover and catalog data schemas in S3, creating a searchable metadata catalog that integrates with Athena, EMR, and SageMaker for downstream ML use.

question: A manufacturing company uses an ML model to determine whether products meet a standard for quality. The model produces an output of Passed or Failed. Robots separate the products into the two categories by using the model to analyze photos on the assembly line. Which metrics should the company use to evaluate the model's performance? (Choose two.)

options.A: Precision and recall
options.B: Root mean square error (RMSE) and mean absolute percentage error (MAPE)
options.C: Accuracy and F1 score
options.D: Bilingual Evaluation Understudy (BLEU) score

explanation: For manufacturing quality inspection: F1 Score balances precision and recall for the binary pass/fail classification. A confusion matrix reveals the specific error patterns (false positives vs. false negatives).

question: An ML engineer needs to encrypt all data in transit when an ML training job runs. The ML engineer must ensure that encryption in transit is applied to processes that Amazon SageMaker uses during the training job. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Encrypt communication between nodes for batch processing.
options.B: Encrypt communication between nodes in a training cluster.
options.C: Specify an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key during creation of the training job request.
options.D: Specify an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key during creation of the SageMaker domain.

explanation: Enabling inter-container traffic encryption (EnableInterContainerTrafficEncryption=true) in SageMaker encrypts all node-to-node communication during distributed training with TLS.

question: An ML engineer needs to use metrics to assess the quality of a time-series forecasting model. Which metrics apply to this model? (Choose two.)

options.A: Recall
options.B: LogLoss
options.C: Root mean square error (RMSE)
options.D: InferenceLatency

explanation: WAPE (Weighted Absolute Percentage Error) and RMSE are standard time-series forecasting metrics — WAPE measures relative error magnitude, RMSE penalizes large prediction errors.

question: A company runs Amazon SageMaker ML models that use accelerated instances. The models require real-time responses. Each model has different scaling requirements. The company must not allow a cold start for the models. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create a SageMaker Serverless Inference endpoint for each model. Use provisioned concurrency for the endpoints.
options.B: Create a SageMaker Asynchronous Inference endpoint for each model. Create an auto scaling policy for each endpoint.
options.C: Create a SageMaker endpoint. Create an inference component for each model. In the inference component settings, specify the newly created endpoint. Create an auto scaling policy for each inference component. Set the parameter for the minimum number of copies to at least 1.
options.D: Create an Amazon S3 bucket. Store all the model artifacts in the S3 bucket. Create a SageMaker multi-model endpoint. Point the endpoint to the S3 bucket. Create an auto scaling policy for the endpoint. Set the parameter for the minimum number of copies to at least 1.

explanation: Amazon Elastic Inference (now replaced by Inf1/Inf2 instances) attaches GPU acceleration to any instance type, providing cost-effective inference acceleration for deep learning models.

question: A company uses Amazon SageMaker for its ML process. A compliance audit discovers that an Amazon S3 bucket for training data uses server-side encryption with S3 managed keys (SSE-S3). The company requires customer managed keys. An ML engineer changes the S3 bucket to use server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS). After the change to the encryption settings, SageMaker training jobs start to fail with AccessDenied errors. What should the ML engineer do to resolve this problem?

options.A: Update the IAM policy that is attached to the execution role for the training jobs. Include the s3:ListBucket and s3:GetObject permissions.
options.B: Update the S3 bucket policy that is attached to the S3 bucket. Set the value of the aws:SecureTransport condition key to True.
options.C: Update the IAM policy that is attached to the execution role for the training jobs. Include the kms:Encrypt and kms:Decrypt permissions.
options.D: Update the IAM policy that is attached to the user that created the training jobs. Include the kms:CreateGrant permission.

explanation: When an S3 bucket used for ML data lacks encryption, apply SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS default encryption and update bucket policies to deny unencrypted uploads (aws:SecureTransport condition).

question: A company runs training jobs on Amazon SageMaker by using a compute optimized instance. Demand for training runs will remain constant for the next 55 weeks. The instance needs to run for 35 hours each week. The company needs to reduce its model training costs. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use a serverless endpoint with a provisioned concurrency of 35 hours for each week. Run the training on the endpoint.
options.B: Use SageMaker Edge Manager for the training. Specify the instance requirement in the edge device configuration. Run the training.
options.C: Use the heterogeneous cluster feature of SageMaker Training. Configure the instance_type, instance_count, and instance_groups arguments to run training jobs.
options.D: Opt in to a SageMaker Savings Plan with a 1-year term and an All Upfront payment. Run a SageMaker Training job on the instance.

explanation: SageMaker Savings Plans provide up to 64% savings over On-Demand pricing for consistent SageMaker usage, applying automatically to training, inference, and notebook instance hours.

question: A company deployed an ML model that uses the XGBoost algorithm to predict product failures. The model is hosted on an Amazon SageMaker endpoint and is trained on normal operating data. An AWS Lambda function provides the predictions to the company's application. An ML engineer must implement a solution that uses incoming live data to detect decreased model accuracy over time. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon CloudWatch to create a dashboard that monitors real-time inference data and model predictions. Use the dashboard to detect drift.
options.B: Modify the Lambda function to calculate model drift by using real-time inference data and model predictions. Program the Lambda function to send alerts.
options.C: Schedule a monitoring job in SageMaker Model Monitor. Use the job to detect drift by analyzing the live data against a baseline of the training data statistics and constraints.
options.D: Schedule a monitoring job in SageMaker Debugger. Use the job to detect drift by analyzing the live data against a baseline of the training data statistics and constraints.

explanation: Concept drift occurs when the real-world relationship between features and target changes over time. Retraining with recent data updates the model to reflect current patterns.

question: A company has an ML model that uses historical transaction data to predict customer behavior. An ML engineer is optimizing the model in Amazon SageMaker to enhance the model's predictive accuracy. The ML engineer must examine the input data and the resulting predictions to identify trends that could skew the model's performance across different demographics. Which solution will provide this level of analysis?

options.A: Use Amazon CloudWatch to monitor network metrics and CPU metrics for resource optimization during model training.
options.B: Create AWS Glue DataBrew recipes to correct the data based on statistics from the model output.
options.C: Use SageMaker Clarify to evaluate the model and training data for underlying patterns that might affect accuracy.
options.D: Create AWS Lambda functions to automate data pre-processing and to ensure consistent quality of input data for the model.

explanation: SageMaker Model Monitor's data quality monitoring compares incoming feature distributions against a baseline, detecting statistical drift using constraints and CloudWatch alerts.

question: A company uses 10 Reserved Instances of accelerated instance types to serve the current version of an ML model. An ML engineer needs to deploy a new version of the model to an Amazon SageMaker real-time inference endpoint. The solution must use the original 10 instances to serve both versions of the model. The solution also must include one additional Reserved Instance that is available to use in the deployment process. The transition between versions must occur with no downtime or service interruptions. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Configure a blue/green deployment with all-at-once traffic shifting.
options.B: Configure a blue/green deployment with canary traffic shifting and a size of 10%.
options.C: Configure a shadow test with a traffic sampling percentage of 10%.
options.D: Configure a rolling deployment with a rolling batch size of 1.

explanation: Blue/green deployment creates a new endpoint with the updated model, validates it, then shifts traffic. Reserved Instances continue serving during the transition, minimizing downtime.

question: An IoT company uses Amazon SageMaker to train and test an XGBoost model for object detection. ML engineers need to monitor performance metrics when they train the model with variants in hyperparameters. The ML engineers also need to send Short Message Service (SMS) text messages after training is complete. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon CloudWatch to monitor performance metrics. Use Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) for message delivery.
options.B: Use Amazon CloudWatch to monitor performance metrics. Use Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) for message delivery.
options.C: Use AWS CloudTrail to monitor performance metrics. Use Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) for message delivery.
options.D: Use AWS CloudTrail to monitor performance metrics. Use Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) for message delivery.

explanation: Reducing max_depth and increasing min_child_weight in XGBoost constrains tree complexity, reducing overfitting while maintaining the model's ability to capture important patterns.

question: A company is working on an ML project that will include Amazon SageMaker notebook instances. An ML engineer must ensure that the SageMaker notebook instances do not allow root access. Which solution will prevent the deployment of notebook instances that allow root access?

options.A: Use IAM condition keys to stop deployments of SageMaker notebook instances that allow root access.
options.B: Use AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) keys to stop deployments of SageMaker notebook instances that allow root access.
options.C: Monitor resource creation by using Amazon EventBridge events. Create an AWS Lambda function that deletes all deployed SageMaker notebook instances that allow root access.
options.D: Monitor resource creation by using AWS CloudFormation events. Create an AWS Lambda function that deletes all deployed SageMaker notebook instances that allow root access.

explanation: VPC endpoint policies restrict which AWS services and resources can be accessed through the endpoint, ensuring notebooks can only reach approved S3 buckets and SageMaker APIs.

question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker to develop ML models. The company stores sensitive training data in an Amazon S3 bucket. The model training must have network isolation from the internet. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Run the SageMaker training jobs in private subnets. Create a NAT gateway. Route traffic for training through the NAT gateway.
options.B: Run the SageMaker training jobs in private subnets. Create an S3 gateway VPC endpoint. Route traffic for training through the S3 gateway VPC endpoint.
options.C: Run the SageMaker training jobs in public subnets that have an attached security group. In the security group, use inbound rules to limit traffic from the internet. Encrypt SageMaker instance storage by using server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS).
options.D: Encrypt traffic to Amazon S3 by using a bucket policy that includes a value of True for the aws:SecureTransport condition key. Use default at-rest encryption for Amazon S3. Encrypt SageMaker instance storage by using server-side encryption with AWS KMS keys (SSE-KMS).

explanation: SageMaker notebooks in VPCs with interface endpoints (PrivateLink) keep all API traffic on the AWS backbone. S3 VPC gateway endpoints ensure data never traverses the public internet.

question: A company needs an AWS solution that will automatically create versions of ML models as the models are created. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR)
options.B: Model packages from Amazon SageMaker Marketplace
options.C: Amazon SageMaker ML Lineage Tracking
options.D: Amazon SageMaker Model Registry

explanation: SageMaker Model Registry automatically versions models as model packages within model groups, tracking lineage, metadata, and approval status for each version.

question: A company needs to use Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to supplement an open source large language model (LLM) that runs on Amazon Bedrock. The company's data for RAG is a set of documents in an Amazon S3 bucket. The documents consist of .csv files and .docx files. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Create a pipeline in Amazon SageMaker Pipelines to generate a new model. Call the new model from Amazon Bedrock to perform RAG queries.
options.B: Convert the data into vectors. Store the data in an Amazon Neptune database. Connect the database to Amazon Bedrock. Call the Amazon Bedrock API to perform RAG queries.
options.C: Fine-tune an existing LLM by using an AutoML job in Amazon SageMaker. Configure the S3 bucket as a data source for the AutoML job. Deploy the LLM to a SageMaker endpoint. Use the endpoint to perform RAG queries.
options.D: Create a knowledge base for Amazon Bedrock. Configure a data source that references the S3 bucket. Use the Amazon Bedrock API to perform RAG queries.

explanation: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases with Amazon OpenSearch Serverless provides a fully managed RAG pipeline — handling document ingestion, embedding, vector storage, and retrieval automatically.

question: A company plans to deploy an ML model for production inference on an Amazon SageMaker endpoint. The average inference payload size will vary from 100 MB to 300 MB. Inference requests must be processed in 60 minutes or less. Which SageMaker inference option will meet these requirements?

options.A: Serverless inference
options.B: Asynchronous inference
options.C: Real-time inference
options.D: Batch transform

explanation: SageMaker asynchronous inference handles large payloads (up to 1 GB) and long processing times (up to 60 min), with auto scaling to zero when idle — ideal for sporadic, large-payload workloads.

question: An ML engineer notices class imbalance in an image classification training job. What should the ML engineer do to resolve this issue?

options.A: Reduce the size of the dataset.
options.B: Transform some of the images in the dataset.
options.C: Apply random oversampling on the dataset.
options.D: Apply random data splitting on the dataset.

explanation: Data augmentation (rotation, flipping, cropping, color jittering) artificially increases the minority class representation in image datasets, helping the model learn balanced representations.

question: A company receives daily .csv files about customer interactions with its ML model. The company stores the files in Amazon S3 and uses the files to retrain the model. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to mask credit card numbers in the files before the model is retrained. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST development effort?

options.A: Create a discovery job in Amazon Macie. Configure the job to find and mask sensitive data.
options.B: Create Apache Spark code to run on an AWS Glue job. Use the Sensitive Data Detection functionality in AWS Glue to find and mask sensitive data.
options.C: Create Apache Spark code to run on an AWS Glue job. Program the code to perform a regex operation to find and mask sensitive data.
options.D: Create Apache Spark code to run on an Amazon EC2 instance. Program the code to perform an operation to find and mask sensitive data.

explanation: SageMaker Pipelines orchestrates the full workflow: processing → training → evaluation → registration. EventBridge triggers the pipeline when new data arrives in S3.

question: A medical company is using AWS to build a tool to recommend treatments for patients. The company has obtained health records and self-reported textual information in English from patients. The company needs to use this information to gain insight about the patients. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST development effort?

options.A: Use Amazon SageMaker to build a recurrent neural network (RNN) to summarize the data.
options.B: Use Amazon Comprehend Medical to summarize the data.
options.C: Use Amazon Kendra to create a quick-search tool to query the data.
options.D: Use the Amazon SageMaker Sequence-to-Sequence (seq2seq) algorithm to create a text summary from the data.

explanation: Amazon SageMaker Clarify computes bias metrics and SHAP-based explainability, helping ensure ML models for sensitive applications (healthcare, finance) are fair and interpretable.

question: A company needs to extract entities from a PDF document to build a classifier model. Which solution will extract and store the entities in the LEAST amount of time?

options.A: Use Amazon Comprehend to extract the entities. Store the output in Amazon S3.
options.B: Use an open source AI optical character recognition (OCR) tool on Amazon SageMaker to extract the entities. Store the output in Amazon S3.
options.C: Use Amazon Textract to extract the entities. Use Amazon Comprehend to convert the entities to text. Store the output in Amazon S3.
options.D: Use Amazon Textract integrated with Amazon Augmented AI (Amazon A2I) to extract the entities. Store the output in Amazon S3.

explanation: Amazon Textract extracts text, tables, and forms from PDF/image documents. The extracted structured data can then be used as input features for downstream classifier models.

question: A company shares Amazon SageMaker Studio notebooks that are accessible through a VPN. The company must enforce access controls to prevent malicious actors from exploiting presigned URLs to access the notebooks. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Set up Studio client IP validation by using the aws:sourceIp IAM policy condition.
options.B: Set up Studio client VPC validation by using the aws:sourceVpc IAM policy condition.
options.C: Set up Studio client role endpoint validation by using the aws:PrimaryTag IAM policy condition.
options.D: Set up Studio client user endpoint validation by using the aws:PrincipalTag IAM policy condition.

explanation: SageMaker notebook presigned URLs can be restricted to specific IP ranges or VPN CIDRs using IAM policy conditions (aws:SourceIp), preventing access from unauthorized networks.

question: An ML engineer needs to merge and transform data from two sources to retrain an existing ML model. One data source consists of .csv files that are stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. Each .csv file consists of millions of records. The other data source is an Amazon Aurora DB cluster. The result of the merge process must be written to a second S3 bucket. The ML engineer needs to perform this merge-and-transform task every week. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Create a transient Amazon EMR cluster every week. Use the cluster to run an Apache Spark job to merge and transform the data.
options.B: Create a weekly AWS Glue job that uses the Apache Spark engine. Use DynamicFrame native operations to merge and transform the data.
options.C: Create an AWS Lambda function that runs Apache Spark code every week to merge and transform the data. Configure the Lambda function to connect to the initial S3 bucket and the DB cluster.
options.D: Create an AWS Batch job that runs Apache Spark code on Amazon EC2 instances every week. Configure the Spark code to save the data from the EC2 instances to the second S3 bucket.

explanation: AWS Glue ETL jobs with Apache Spark can read from multiple data sources (S3, JDBC databases), perform joins and transformations, and output clean data for ML training.

question: An ML engineer has deployed an Amazon SageMaker model to a serverless endpoint in production. The model is invoked by the InvokeEndpoint API operation. The model's latency in production is higher than the baseline latency in the test environment. The ML engineer thinks that the increase in latency is because of model startup time. What should the ML engineer do to confirm or deny this hypothesis?

options.A: Schedule a SageMaker Model Monitor job. Observe metrics about model quality.
options.B: Schedule a SageMaker Model Monitor job with Amazon CloudWatch metrics enabled.
options.C: Enable Amazon CloudWatch metrics. Observe the ModelSetupTime metric in the SageMaker namespace.
options.D: Enable Amazon CloudWatch metrics. Observe the ModelLoadingWaitTime metric in the SageMaker namespace.

explanation: The ModelSetupTime CloudWatch metric in the AWS/SageMaker namespace measures cold-start latency for serverless endpoints — the time to provision resources and load the model.

question: An ML engineer needs to ensure that a dataset complies with regulations for personally identifiable information (PII). The ML engineer will use the data to train an ML model on Amazon SageMaker instances. SageMaker must not use any of the PII. Which solution will meet these requirements in the MOST operationally efficient way?

options.A: Use the Amazon Comprehend DetectPiiEntities API call to redact the PII from the data. Store the data in an Amazon S3 bucket. Access the S3 bucket from the SageMaker instances for model training.
options.B: Use the Amazon Comprehend DetectPiiEntities API call to redact the PII from the data. Store the data in an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system. Mount the EFS file system to the SageMaker instances for model training.
options.C: Use AWS Glue DataBrew to cleanse the dataset of PII. Store the data in an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system. Mount the EFS file system to the SageMaker instances for model training.
options.D: Use Amazon Macie for automatic discovery of PII in the data. Remove the PII. Store the data in an Amazon S3 bucket. Mount the S3 bucket to the SageMaker instances for model training.

explanation: Amazon Macie uses ML to automatically discover, classify, and protect sensitive PII data in S3, providing alerts and dashboards for data privacy compliance.

question: A company must install a custom script on any newly created Amazon SageMaker notebook instances. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Create a lifecycle configuration script to install the custom script when a new SageMaker notebook is created. Attach the lifecycle configuration to every new SageMaker notebook as part of the creation steps.
options.B: Create a custom Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) image that contains the custom script. Push the ECR image to a Docker registry. Attach the Docker image to a SageMaker Studio domain. Select the kernel to run as part of the SageMaker notebook.
options.C: Create a custom package index repository. Use AWS CodeArtifact to manage the installation of the custom script. Set up AWS PrivateLink endpoints to connect CodeArtifact to the SageMaker instance. Install the script.
options.D: Store the custom script in Amazon S3. Create an AWS Lambda function to install the custom script on new SageMaker notebooks. Configure Amazon EventBridge to invoke the Lambda function when a new SageMaker notebook is initialized.

explanation: SageMaker lifecycle configurations are shell scripts that run when notebook instances start or are created, enabling automated installation of packages, libraries, or compliance tools.

question: A company is building a real-time data processing pipeline for an ecommerce application. The application generates a high volume of clickstream data that must be ingested, processed, and visualized in near real time. The company needs a solution that supports SQL for data processing and Jupyter notebooks for interactive analysis. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon Data Firehose to ingest the data. Create an AWS Lambda function to process the data. Store the processed data in Amazon S3. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the data.
options.B: Use Amazon Kinesis Data Streams to ingest the data. Use Amazon Data Firehose to transform the data. Use Amazon Athena to process the data. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the data.
options.C: Use Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka (Amazon MSK) to ingest the data. Use AWS Glue with PySpark to process the data. Store the processed data in Amazon S3. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the data.
options.D: Use Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka (Amazon MSK) to ingest the data. Use Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink to process the data. Use the built-in Flink dashboard to visualize the data.

explanation: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink (Studio Notebooks) supports both SQL and Jupyter-style interactive analysis on streaming data — ideal for real-time clickstream analytics.

question: A medical company needs to store clinical data. The data includes personally identifiable information (PII) and protected health information (PHI). An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to ensure that the PII and PHI are not used to train ML models. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Store the clinical data in Amazon S3 buckets. Use AWS Glue DataBrew to mask the PII and PHI before the data is used for model training.
options.B: Upload the clinical data to an Amazon Redshift database. Use built-in SQL stored procedures to automatically classify and mask the PII and PHI before the data is used for model training.
options.C: Use Amazon Comprehend to detect and mask the PII before the data is used for model training. Use Amazon Comprehend Medical to detect and mask the PHI before the data is used for model training.
options.D: Create an AWS Lambda function to encrypt the PII and PHI. Program the Lambda function to save the encrypted data to an Amazon S3 bucket for model training.

explanation: AWS Lake Formation with LF-Tags provides column-level and row-level access control, enabling fine-grained PII protection while allowing authorized analytics on non-sensitive columns.

question: An ML engineer is developing a classification model. The ML engineer needs to use custom libraries in processing jobs, training jobs, and pipelines in Amazon SageMaker. Which solution will provide this functionality with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.A: Manually install the libraries in the SageMaker containers.
options.B: Build a custom Docker container that includes the required libraries. Host the container in Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR). Use the ECR image in the SageMaker jobs and pipelines.
options.C: Create a SageMaker notebook instance to host the jobs. Create an AWS Lambda function to install the libraries on the notebook instance when the notebook instance starts. Configure the SageMaker jobs and pipelines to run on the notebook instance.
options.D: Run code for the libraries externally on Amazon EC2 instances. Store the results in Amazon S3. Import the results into the SageMaker jobs and pipelines.

explanation: SageMaker Training with custom Docker containers lets you package any libraries, frameworks, or dependencies into an ECR image, giving full control over the training environment.

question: An ML engineer is deploying a trained model to an Amazon SageMaker endpoint. The ML engineer needs to receive alerts when data quality issues occur in production. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Configure an Amazon CloudWatch metric alarm and a corresponding action to send an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) notification.
options.B: Integrate the SageMaker endpoint with a SageMaker Clarify processing job. Configure an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to provide alerts.
options.C: Configure a monitoring job in SageMaker Model Monitor. Integrate Model Monitor with Amazon CloudWatch to provide alerts.
options.D: Configure a data flow in SageMaker Data Wrangler. Integrate Data Wrangler with Amazon CloudWatch to provide alerts.

explanation: SageMaker Inference Pipeline chains multiple containers (preprocessor → model → postprocessor) in a single endpoint, ensuring consistent data transformations at inference time.

question: A company needs to use Amazon SageMaker to train a model on more than 300 GB of data. The training data is composed of files that are 200 MB in size. The data is stored in Amazon S3 Standard storage and feeds a dashboard tool. Which SageMaker training ingestion mechanism is the MOST cost-effective solution for this scenario?

options.A: Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system
options.B: Amazon FSx for Lustre file system
options.C: Amazon S3 in fast file mode while using S3 Express One Zone
options.D: Amazon S3 in fast file mode without using S3 Express One Zone

explanation: SageMaker File mode downloads all training data to the instance's local storage before training starts. For 300+ GB datasets, this ensures the data is available for random access during training.

question: A company has an ML model that is deployed to an Amazon SageMaker endpoint for real-time inference. The company needs to deploy a new model. The company must compare the new model's performance to the currently deployed model's performance before shifting all traffic to the new model. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational effort?

options.A: Deploy the new model to a separate endpoint. Manually split traffic between the two endpoints.
options.B: Deploy the new model to a separate endpoint. Use Amazon CloudFront to distribute traffic between the two endpoints.
options.C: Deploy the new model as a shadow variant on the same endpoint as the current model. Route a portion of live traffic to the shadow model for evaluation.
options.D: Use AWS Lambda functions with custom logic to route traffic between the current model and the new model.

explanation: SageMaker endpoint auto scaling with target tracking on InvocationsPerInstance automatically adds or removes instances based on traffic, handling burst patterns while maintaining latency targets.

question: A company runs an ML model on Amazon SageMaker. The company uses an automatic process that makes API calls to create training jobs for the model. The company has new compliance rules that prohibit the collection of aggregated metadata from training jobs. Which solution will prevent SageMaker from collecting metadata from the training jobs?

options.A: Opt out of metadata tracking for any training job that is submitted.
options.B: Ensure that training jobs are running in a private subnet in a custom VPC.
options.C: Encrypt the training data with an AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed key.
options.D: Reconfigure the training jobs to use only AWS Nitro instances.

explanation: AWS CloudTrail logs all API calls (including CreateEndpoint, CreateModel) with timestamps, IAM identity, and parameters — providing a complete audit trail for compliance and debugging.

question: A company is exploring generative AI and wants to add a new product feature. An ML engineer is making API calls from existing Amazon EC2 instances to Amazon Bedrock. The EC2 instances are in a private subnet and must remain private during the implementation. The EC2 instances have an assigned security group that allows access to all IP addresses in the private subnet. What should the ML engineer do to establish a connection between the EC2 instances and Amazon Bedrock?

options.A: Modify the security group to allow inbound and outbound traffic to and from Amazon Bedrock.
options.B: Use AWS PrivateLink to access Amazon Bedrock through an interface VPC endpoint.
options.C: Configure Amazon Bedrock to use the private subnet where the EC2 instances are deployed.
options.D: Link the existing VPC to Amazon Bedrock by using an AWS Direct Connect connection.

explanation: Amazon Bedrock provides access to multiple foundation models (Claude, Llama, Titan) via a unified API, enabling rapid prototyping of generative AI features without managing infrastructure.

question: A company wants to launch a new internal generative AI interface to answer user questions. The interface will be based on a popular open source large language model (LLM). Which combination of steps will deploy the interface with the LEAST operational overhead? (Choose two.)

options.A: Use Amazon SageMaker JumpStart to deploy the LLM.
options.B: Download the LLM as a .zip file. Deploy the LLM on a GPU-based Amazon EC2 instance.
options.C: Create a frontend HTML interface that uses an Amazon API Gateway WebSocket API with AWS Lambda functions to handle the user interaction.
options.D: Use Amazon QuickSight to create a UI to handle the user interaction.
options.E: Use Amazon Lex to create a UI to handle the user interaction.

explanation: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) grounds LLM responses in company-specific data. Guardrails filter harmful content. Together they ensure accurate, safe internal AI assistants.

question: A company wants to build a real-time analytics application that uses streaming data from social media. An ML engineer must implement a solution that ingests and transforms 5 GB of data each minute. The solution also must load the data into a data store that supports fast queries for the real-time analytics. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon EventBridge to ingest the social media data. Use AWS Glue to transform the data. Store the transformed data in Amazon ElastiCache (Memcached).
options.B: Use Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) to ingest the social media data. Use AWS Lambda to transform the data. Store the transformed data in Amazon S3.
options.C: Use Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) to ingest the social media data. Use Amazon EMR to transform the data. Store the transformed data in Amazon RDS.
options.D: Use Amazon Kinesis Data Streams to ingest the social media data. Use Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink to transform the data. Store the transformed data in Amazon DynamoDB.

explanation: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink processes streaming data from Kinesis in real time with sub-second latency, ideal for continuous social media analytics dashboards.

question: A company stores training data as a .csv file in an Amazon S3 bucket. The company must encrypt the data and must control which applications have access to the encryption key. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create a new SSH access key. Use the AWS Encryption CLI with a reference to the new access key to encrypt the file.
options.B: Create a new API key by using the Amazon API Gateway CreateApiKey API operation. Use the AWS CLI with a reference to the new API key to encrypt the file.
options.C: Create a new IAM role. Attach a policy that allows the AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) GenerateDataKey action. Use the role to encrypt the file.
options.D: Create a new AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key. Use the AWS Encryption CLI with a reference to the new KMS key to encrypt the file.

explanation: SSE-KMS with a customer-managed key (CMK) gives the company full control over encryption key rotation, access policies, and audit logging via CloudTrail — meeting strict compliance needs.

question: A company needs to perform feature engineering, aggregation, and data preparation. After the features are produced, the company must implement a solution on AWS to process and store the features. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon SageMaker Feature Processing to process and ingest the data. Use SageMaker Feature Store to manage and store the features.
options.B: Use Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor to automatically ingest and transform the data. Create an Amazon S3 bucket to store the features in JSON format.
options.C: Use Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink to transform the data and to ingest the data directly into Amazon SageMaker Feature Store. Use Feature Store to manage and store the features.
options.D: Use an Amazon SageMaker batch transform job to analyze, transform, and ingest the data. Create an Amazon DynamoDB table to store the features.

explanation: SageMaker Feature Store provides both offline (batch) and online (low-latency) feature access, ensuring consistent feature values across training and real-time inference pipelines.

question: A company is developing a new online application to gather information from customers. An ML engineer has developed a new ML model that will determine a score for each customer. The model will use the score to determine which product to display to the customer. The ML engineer needs to minimize response-time latency for the model. How should the ML engineer deploy the application in Amazon SageMaker to meet these requirements?

options.A: Configure batch transform.
options.B: Configure a real-time inference endpoint.
options.C: Configure a serverless inference endpoint.
options.D: Configure an asynchronous inference endpoint.

explanation: Amazon Comprehend detects PII entities (names, addresses, SSNs) in text. Running Comprehend on incoming data before storage enables automated PII detection and redaction.

question: A company is using Amazon EMR. The company has a large dataset in Amazon S3 that needs to be ingested into Amazon SageMaker Feature Store. The dataset contains historical data and real-time streaming data. The company must ensure that the Feature Store online store is updated with the most recent data as soon as the data becomes available. The company also must maintain a complete Feature Store offline store for batch processing. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use the PutRecord API in Feature Store Runtime to ingest all the data into the online store.
options.B: Use the PutRecord API in Feature Store Runtime to ingest all the data into the offline store.
options.C: Use the Feature Store Spark connector to ingest the data as Spark DataFrames with the online store and offline store enabled.
options.D: Use the Feature Store Spark connector to ingest the data as Spark DataFrames with only the online store enabled.

explanation: S3DistCp (distributed copy) efficiently transfers large datasets between S3 and HDFS on EMR clusters, optimizing for parallel copying and supporting compression/decompression.

question: An ML engineer needs to deploy four ML models in an Amazon SageMaker inference pipeline. The models were built with different frameworks. The ML engineer also needs to give clients the ability to use the invoke_endpoint call to perform inference for each model. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

options.A: Create a SageMaker multi-model endpoint.
options.B: Create a SageMaker multi-container endpoint.
options.C: Create multiple SageMaker single-model endpoints.
options.D: Run a SparkML job to generate multiple endpoints.

explanation: SageMaker multi-container endpoints with Inference Pipeline chain up to 15 containers sequentially, processing data through each container in order for complex inference workflows.

question: An ML engineer wants an Amazon SageMaker notebook to automatically stop running after 1 hour of idle time. How can the ML engineer accomplish this goal?

options.A: Create a lifecycle configuration in SageMaker. Copy the auto-stop-idle script from GitHub to the Start Notebook section.
options.B: Create a lifecycle configuration in SageMaker. Copy the auto-stop-idle script from GitHub to the Create Notebook section.
options.C: Track the notebook's CPU metric by using Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Invoke an AWS Lambda function from CloudWatch Logs to shut down the notebook instance if CPU utilization becomes zero.
options.D: Track the notebook's memory metric by using Amazon CloudWatch Logs. Invoke an AWS Lambda function from CloudWatch Logs to shut down the notebook instance if memory utilization becomes zero.

explanation: SageMaker lifecycle configurations with idle-checking scripts can automatically stop notebook instances after a specified idle period, reducing costs from forgotten running instances.

question: A company wants to provide services to help other businesses label images. The company wants its labeling specialists to complete human labeling tasks on AWS. How should the company register the labeling specialists to receive tasks on AWS?

options.A: Use AWS Data Exchange.
options.B: Create and use an internal workforce in Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth.
options.C: Create and use Amazon Mechanical Turk entities in an Amazon SageMaker human loop.
options.D: Use the Amazon Mechanical Turk website.

explanation: SageMaker Ground Truth with a private workforce lets you build a managed labeling team with custom UI templates, quality controls, and annotation workflows for image labeling at scale.

question: A company wants to use Amazon SageMaker to host an ML model that runs on CPU for real-time predictions. The model will have intermittent traffic during business hours and will have periods of no traffic after business hours. The company needs a solution that will serve inference requests in the most cost-effective manner. Which hosting option will meet these requirements?

options.A: Deploy the model to a SageMaker real-time endpoint. Add a schedule-based auto scaling policy to handle traffic surges during business hours.
options.B: Deploy the model to a SageMaker Serverless Inference endpoint. Configure increased provisioned concurrency during business hours.
options.C: Deploy the model to a SageMaker Asynchronous Inference endpoint. Configure an auto scaling policy that scales in to zero outside business hours.
options.D: Deploy the model to a SageMaker real-time endpoint. Create a scheduled AWS Lambda function that activates the endpoint during business hours only.

explanation: SageMaker multi-model endpoints (MME) host hundreds of models on shared instances, dynamically loading models from S3 on demand — maximizing resource utilization for many similar models.

question: An ML engineer needs to train a supervised deep learning model. The available dataset is a large number of unlabeled images that only employees should access. The ML engineer needs to implement a solution that labels the dataset with the highest possible accuracy. Which combination of steps should the ML engineer take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

options.A: Use Amazon Rekognition to automatically label the dataset.
options.B: Train the deep learning model directly on the raw data. Let the model infer the labels by itself.
options.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth to create an annotation job that specifies the labeling task and requirements.
options.D: Set up workforce teams to access a private workforce to run and review the annotation job created by Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth.
options.E: Use Amazon Mechanical Turk to complete the annotation job created by Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth.

explanation: Stratified sampling preserves class distribution when splitting data. Data augmentation artificially increases samples — both techniques help supervised deep learning with imbalanced classes.

question: A company is using an Amazon S3 bucket to collect data that will be used for ML workflows. The company needs to use AWS Glue DataBrew to clean and normalize the data. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create a DataBrew dataset by using the S3 path. Clean and normalize the data by using a DataBrew profile job.
options.B: Create a DataBrew dataset by using the S3 path. Clean and normalize the data by using a DataBrew recipe job.
options.C: Create a DataBrew dataset by using a Java Database Connectivity (JDBC) driver to connect to the S3 bucket. Clean and normalize the data by using a DataBrew profile job.
options.D: Create a DataBrew dataset by using a Java Database Connectivity (JDBC) driver to connect to the S3 bucket. Clean and normalize the data by using a DataBrew recipe job.

explanation: S3 Object Lock with compliance mode prevents any user (including root) from deleting or overwriting objects during the retention period, ensuring ML training data immutability.

question: A company is developing a new ML model that uses the XGBoost algorithm. The company will train the model on data that is stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. The data is in a nested JSON format. An ML engineer needs to convert the JSON files into a tabular format. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Create an AWS Glue PySpark job that uses the Relationalize transform to convert the files.
options.B: Write custom Scala code to convert the files. Use Amazon EMR Serverless to run the Scala code.
options.C: Create an AWS Lambda function that uses a Python runtime and invokes the reduce() function to convert the files. Invoke the Lambda function.
options.D: Create an Amazon Athena database that is based on the JSON files. Use the Athena flatten function to convert the data.

explanation: SageMaker Automatic Model Tuning runs multiple training jobs with different hyperparameter values, using Bayesian optimization to efficiently find the best-performing configuration.

question: A medical company ingests streams of data from devices that monitor patients' vital signs. The company uses Amazon SageMaker and plans to prepare ML models to predict adverse events for patients. The dataset is large with thousands of features. An ML engineer needs to run several hundred training iterations with different sets of features, different algorithms, and many potential parameters. The ML engineer must implement a solution to log the characteristics and results of each training iteration. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST implementation effort?

options.A: Use Amazon CloudWatch to create custom metrics for the characteristics of each iteration.
options.B: Write the characteristics of each iteration to logs in Amazon S3. Use AWS Glue and Amazon Athena to search the logs.
options.C: Use the SageMaker Model Registry to track the characteristics and results of each iteration.
options.D: Use SageMaker Experiments to track the characteristics and results of each iteration.

explanation: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams captures real-time streaming data. SageMaker real-time endpoints provide sub-second inference — together they enable real-time anomaly detection on vital signs.

question: A company is planning to create an internal-only chat interface to help employees handle customer queries. Currently, the employees need to refer to a massive knowledge base of internal documents to address customer issues. The new solution must be serverless. Which combination of steps will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

options.A: Set up Amazon Bedrock with the Anthropic Claude foundation model.
options.B: Set up Amazon SageMaker JumpStart with the Llama foundation model.
options.C: Use Amazon EC2 instances with Amazon API Gateway to invoke the model API.
options.D: Use AWS Lambda functions with Amazon API Gateway to invoke the model API.
options.E: Use an Amazon S3 bucket to store vector database dumps and embeddings.
options.F: Use Amazon RDS for MySQL to store vector database dumps and embeddings.

explanation: RAG with Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases retrieves company-specific documents to ground responses. Guardrails with denied topics and content filters prevent off-topic or harmful outputs.

question: An ML engineer needs to deploy a trained model that is based on a genetic algorithm. The algorithm solves a complex problem and can take several minutes to generate predictions. When the model is deployed, the model needs to access large amounts of data to process requests. The requests can involve as much as 100 MB of data. Which deployment solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Deploy the model to Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer.
options.B: Deploy the model to an Amazon SageMaker real-time endpoint.
options.C: Deploy the model to an Amazon SageMaker Asynchronous Inference endpoint.
options.D: Package the model as a container. Deploy the model to Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) on Amazon EC2 instances.

explanation: SageMaker endpoints support custom containers via ECR, allowing you to deploy any algorithm (including genetic algorithms) by packaging it in a Docker container with the SageMaker inference toolkit.

question: An ML engineer wants to use a set of survey responses as training data for an ML classifier. All the survey responses are either yes or no. The ML engineer needs to convert the responses into a feature that will produce better model training results. The ML engineer must not increase the dimensionality of the dataset. Which methods will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)

options.A: Binary encoding
options.B: Label encoding
options.C: One-hot encoding
options.D: Statistical imputation

explanation: One-hot encoding converts categorical survey responses into binary vectors. Ordinal encoding preserves the natural ordering of ranked responses (e.g., Strongly Disagree → Strongly Agree).

question: A company has several teams that have developed separate prediction models on their own laptops. The teams developed the models by using Python with scikit-learn and TensorFlow frameworks. The company must rebuild the models and must integrate the models into an ML infrastructure that the company manages by using Amazon SageMaker. The company also must incorporate the models into a model registry. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Export the models from the laptops to an Amazon S3 bucket. Use an Amazon API Gateway REST API and AWS Lambda functions with SageMaker endpoints to access the models. Register the models in the SageMaker Model Registry.
options.B: Import the models into the SageMaker Model Registry. Use SageMaker to run the imported models.
options.C: Use code from the laptops to create containers for the models. Use the bring your own container (BYOC) functionality of SageMaker to import and use the models. Register the models in the SageMaker Model Registry.
options.D: Import the Python-based models into SageMaker. Rebuild the scikit-learn and TensorFlow models in SageMaker. Register all the models in the SageMaker Model Registry.

explanation: SageMaker Studio provides a unified IDE where teams can share notebooks, experiments, and models. SageMaker Projects add MLOps templates for standardized, reproducible ML workflows.

question: A company is training a large language model (LLM) by using on-premises infrastructure. A live conversational engine uses the LLM to help customers find real-time insights in credit card data. An ML engineer must implement a solution to train and deploy the LLM on Amazon SageMaker. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use SageMaker Training Compiler to train the LLM. Deploy the LLM by using SageMaker real-time inference.
options.B: Use SageMaker with deep learning containers for large model inference to train the LLM. Deploy the LLM by using SageMaker real-time inference.
options.C: Use SageMaker Notebook Jobs to train the LLM. Deploy the LLM by using SageMaker Asynchronous Inference.
options.D: Use SageMaker Studio to train the LLM. Deploy the LLM by using SageMaker batch transform.

explanation: Amazon Bedrock provides fully managed access to foundation models with built-in streaming support, eliminating the need to manage GPU infrastructure for real-time LLM inference.

question: A company has an existing Amazon SageMaker model (v1) on a production endpoint. The company develops a new model version (v2) and needs to test v2 in production before substituting v2 for v1. The company needs to implement a solution to minimize the risk of v2 generating incorrect output in production. The solution must prevent any disruption of production traffic during the change to v2. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create a second production variant for v2. Assign 1% of the traffic to v2 and 99% of the traffic to v1. Collect all the output of v2 in an Amazon S3 bucket. If v2 performs as expected, switch all the traffic to v2.
options.B: Create a second production variant for v2. Assign 10% of the traffic to v2 and 90% of the traffic to v1. Collect all the output of v2 in an Amazon S3 bucket. If v2 performs as expected, switch all the traffic to v2.
options.C: Deploy v2 to a new endpoint. Turn on data capturing for the production endpoint. Write a script to pass 100% of input data to v2. If v2 performs as expected, deactivate the v1 endpoint and direct the traffic to v2.
options.D: Deploy v2 into a shadow variant that samples 100% of the inference requests. Collect all the output in an Amazon S3 bucket. If v2 performs as expected, promote v2 to production.

explanation: SageMaker deployment guardrails with canary or linear traffic shifting gradually routes traffic to the new model version, automatically rolling back if CloudWatch alarms trigger.

question: A company is building an ML model by using Amazon SageMaker, AWS owned libraries, and open source libraries. The company must ensure that SageMaker does not collect metadata about usage and errors during training. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Associate the SageMaker domain with a custom IAM role. Attach the role to a policy that denies Amazon CloudWatch service usage logs.
options.B: Add an IAM role to the SageMaker domain to deny Amazon CloudWatch the permission to report metadata.
options.C: Turn off the setting in the SageMaker domain to share metadata for console jobs. Opt out of metadata collection for each training job that is submitted through the AWS CLI or AWS SDKs.
options.D: Set a parameter to opt out of metadata collection for each training job that is submitted through the AWS CLI, Boto3, or the SageMaker Python SDK.

explanation: SageMaker Software Update Tracker (or dependency management via conda/pip in lifecycle configs) helps track and update open-source library versions for security and compatibility.

question: An ML engineer is training an ML model to identify people's health risk based on 20 features and 1 target. The target class has two values: Likely to have health risk (positive class) and Unlikely to have health risk (negative class). The age range of people in the dataset is 30 years old to 60 years old. Age is one of the features. The ML engineer analyzes the features. For the positive class, the difference in proportions of labels (DPL) value is (+0.9) for the age range of 40 to 45 compared with all other age ranges. What should the ML engineer do to correct this data imbalance?

options.A: Oversample the positive class for the age range of 40 to 45.
options.B: Undersample the positive class for the age range of 40 to 45.
options.C: Undersample the positive class for all age ranges except 40 to 45.
options.D: Oversample the negative class for all age ranges except 40 to 45.

explanation: Ablation studies systematically remove features one at a time and measure impact on model performance, identifying which features contribute most and which add noise.

question: A company is building an Amazon SageMaker AI pipeline for an ML model. The pipeline uses distributed processing and training. An ML engineer needs to encrypt network communication between instances that run distributed jobs. The ML engineer configures the distributed jobs to run in a private VPC. What should the ML engineer do to meet the encryption requirement?

options.A: Enable network isolation.
options.B: Configure traffic encryption by using security groups.
options.C: Enable inter-container traffic encryption.
options.D: Enable VPC flow logs.

explanation: SageMaker distributed data parallelism (SMDDP) splits training data across multiple GPU instances, synchronizing gradients efficiently — purpose-built for large-scale distributed training.

question: An ML model is deployed in production. The model has performed well and has met its metric thresholds for months. An ML engineer who is monitoring the model observes a sudden degradation. The performance metrics of the model are now below the thresholds. What could be the cause of the performance degradation?

options.A: Lack of training data
options.B: Drift in production data distribution
options.C: Compute resource constraints
options.D: Model overfitting

explanation: SageMaker Model Monitor compares current data distributions against a baseline. When drift is detected, it triggers CloudWatch alarms that can initiate automated retraining pipelines.

question: An ML engineer is using AWS Glue to transform proprietary data from a third-party vendor to a format that the ML engineer intends to use with the Amazon SageMaker DeepAR forecasting algorithm. The data includes several similar time series data files that the ML engineer must convert to the appropriate format. The ML engineer must compress the files to optimize storage costs. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Snappy to convert the files to RecordIO-Protobuf and to compress the files.
options.B: Use XZ to convert the files to RecordIO-Protobuf and to compress the files.
options.C: Use XZ to convert the files to Apache Parquet format and to compress the files.
options.D: Use gzip to convert the files to Apache Parquet and to compress the files.

explanation: AWS Glue connection properties store JDBC credentials in the Glue Data Catalog, providing secure, managed connectivity to external databases for ETL jobs.

question: A company has significantly increased the amount of data that is stored as .csv files in an Amazon S3 bucket. Data transformation scripts and queries are now taking much longer than they used to take. An ML engineer must implement a solution to optimize the data for query performance. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Configure an AWS Lambda function to split the .csv files into smaller objects in the S3 bucket.
options.B: Configure an AWS Glue job to drop columns that have string type values and to save the results to the S3 bucket.
options.C: Configure an AWS Glue extract, transform, and load (ETL) job to convert the .csv files to Apache Parquet format.
options.D: Configure an Amazon EMR cluster to process the data that is in the S3 bucket.

explanation: Converting CSV to Parquet reduces storage costs (columnar compression) and dramatically improves query performance with Athena due to column pruning and predicate pushdown.

question: An ML engineer is analyzing a classification dataset before training a model in Amazon SageMaker AI. The ML engineer suspects that the dataset has a significant imbalance between class labels that could lead to biased model predictions. To confirm class imbalance, the ML engineer needs to select an appropriate pre-training bias metric. Which metric will meet this requirement?

options.A: Mean square error (MSE)
options.B: Difference in proportions of labels (DPL)
options.C: Silhouette score
options.D: Structural similarity index measure (SSIM)

explanation: In classification, an irrelevant feature (no predictive power) adds noise and increases dimensionality. Removing it improves model performance and reduces training time.

question: An ML engineer uses one ML framework to train multiple ML models. The ML engineer needs to optimize the inference costs and host the models on Amazon SageMaker AI. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

options.A: Create a multi-container inference endpoint for direct invocation.
options.B: Create a multi-model inference endpoint for all the models.
options.C: Create a multi-container inference endpoint for sequential invocation.
options.D: Create multiple single-model inference endpoint for each model.

explanation: SageMaker Training Compiler optimizes DL model training by compiling the computation graph, reducing training time by up to 50% without code changes for supported frameworks.

question: A company is using an ML model to classify motion in videos. The data is stored in MP4 format in Amazon S3. When the company created the model, the company needed 4 months to label all the video frames. The company needs to retrain the model with an existing training workflow in Amazon SageMaker AI. An ML engineer must implement a solution that decreases the labeling time. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use SageMaker Ground Truth to annotate the video frames.
options.B: Use SageMaker JumpStart to use pre-trained computer vision models to develop a labeling model.
options.C: Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to create a data workflow. Use the workflow to optimize the labeling process.
options.D: Use the labeling interface of Amazon Augmented AI (Amazon A2I) with Amazon Rekognition to label the video frames.

explanation: For video classification, frame extraction (sampling frames from MP4) converts video into image sequences, which can then be processed by image classification or action recognition models.

question: An ecommerce company trains an ML model to forecast demand for near real-time inventory management based on historical customer activity. The company successfully deploys the trained model to a production Amazon SageMaker AI endpoint. However, the company notices that the model's forecast performance degrades over time. The company needs a long-term and automated solution to mitigate the performance degradation. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon SageMaker Debugger to automatically send alerts when model performance anomalies are detected.
options.B: Use AWS X-Ray to monitor the performance of the SageMaker AI endpoint and the incoming requests to inform model re-training.
options.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth to curate a high-quality dataset. Use the dataset to re-train the model.
options.D: Use Amazon SageMaker Clarify to monitor model and feature attribution bias to inform model re-training.

explanation: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams with SageMaker real-time endpoints provides sub-second streaming inference — essential for near real-time demand forecasting and inventory management.

question: A logistics company has installed in-vehicle cameras for basic monitoring of its drivers. The company wants to improve driver safety by identifying distractions that could lead to accidents. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational effort?

options.A: Use Amazon Rekognition eye gaze direction detection to monitor driver behavior and identify distractions.
options.B: Use Amazon SageMaker AI to customize an AI model to monitor driver behavior and identify distractions.
options.C: Integrate a third-party driver monitoring system with Amazon Rekognition to monitor driver behavior and identify distractions.
options.D: Use Amazon Comprehend to analyze text-based driver feedback and identify distractions.

explanation: Amazon Rekognition provides pre-trained computer vision APIs for face detection, object detection, and video analysis — enabling rapid deployment without custom model training.

question: A company has trained an ML model that is packaged in a container. The company will integrate the model with an existing Python web application. The company needs to host the model on AWS by using Kubernetes. The company does not want to manage the control plane and must provision the resources in a repeatable manner. The infrastructure must be provisioned by using Python. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use AWS CloudFormation to provision Amazon EC2 instances in multiple Availability Zones. Set up a Kubernetes cluster. Host the model container on the Kubernetes cluster.
options.B: Use the AWS CLI to provision an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster. Store the image in an Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) repository. Host the model container on the EKS cluster.
options.C: Use the AWS Cloud Development Kit (AWS CDK) to provision an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster. Store the image in an Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) repository. Host the model container on the EKS cluster.
options.D: Use AWS CloudFormation to provision an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster. Store the image in an Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) repository. Host the model container on the EKS cluster.

explanation: SageMaker multi-container endpoints allow chaining independent models (e.g., object detection → classification) in a direct invocation pattern, each in its own container.

question: An ML engineer is developing a linear regression ML model. The model shows high accuracy on the training dataset but performs poorly on unseen new data. Which action should the ML engineer take to address this issue?

options.A: Increase the complexity of the model to capture more patterns in the training data. Use Amazon SageMaker Debugger to monitor for convergence issues.
options.B: Apply ML techniques such as cross-validation and regularization. Use Amazon SageMaker Experiments to track and compare different model versions and their performance metrics.
options.C: Directly deploy the model into production. Use Amazon SageMaker Clarify to interpret model outputs on new data. Adjust the model based on these insights.
options.D: Increase the size of the training dataset without adjusting the size of the model. Retrain the model on the new data. Generate a confusion matrix to analyze the results.

explanation: High training accuracy with low validation accuracy is classic overfitting. L2 regularization (weight decay) penalizes large weights, constraining model complexity and improving generalization.

question: A company is training a new ML model to replace a model that is deployed on an Amazon SageMaker AI real-time endpoint. An ML engineer needs to determine the latency and the accuracy of the new model. The ML engineer must evaluate the new model in a production scenario without affecting the users of the existing model. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Perform a blue/green deployment with linear traffic shifting.
options.B: Perform a blue/green deployment with canary traffic shifting.
options.C: Perform a rolling deployment with a rolling batch size of 50% of the current fleet.
options.D: Perform shadow testing with a traffic sampling percentage of 100%.

explanation: SageMaker shadow testing mirrors live production traffic to a shadow variant, comparing new model predictions against the current model without affecting real users or latency.

question: An ML engineer wants to use, prepare, and load data from Amazon S3 for analytics. The ML engineer must run an extract, transform, and load (ETL) job to discover the schema of the data and to store the metadata. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST manual effort?

options.A: Use AWS Glue to run the ETL job. Use the job to discover the schema and to store the associated metadata in the AWS Glue Data Catalog.
options.B: Create an Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler flow to run the ETL job. Use the job to discover the schema and to store the associated metadata in an S3 bucket.
options.C: Create an ETL pipeline by using Amazon Athena integrated with AWS Step Functions. Use the pipeline to run the ETL job to discover the schema and to store the associated metadata in an S3 bucket.
options.D: Launch an Amazon EC2 instance that includes the scikit-learn library to run the ETL job. Use the job to discover the schema and to store the associated metadata in Amazon Redshift.

explanation: AWS Glue DataBrew provides a visual, no-code interface to profile, clean, and transform S3 data with 250+ built-in transformations — ideal for preparing data for analytics and ML.

question: An ML engineer is building an ML pipeline. The pipeline must process a dataset in two ways by using Amazon Athena. The pipeline must use batch processing to perform large-scale data transformations and for model training. The pipeline must also use near real-time processing to perform low-latency queries for inference and analytics. Which file format will provide the LEAST latency for both types of processing?

options.A: CSV
options.B: Apache Parquet
options.C: Nested JSON
options.D: Deserialized JSON

explanation: SageMaker Pipelines supports conditional branching (ConditionStep) to route workflow execution based on evaluation metrics, enabling different processing paths within a single pipeline.

question: A company uses an Amazon SageMaker AI ML model to make real-time inferences. The company has configured auto scaling for the Amazon EC2 instances that SageMaker AI uses for the inferences. During times of peak usage, new instances launch before existing instances are fully ready. As a result, the model experiences inefficiencies and delays. Which solution will optimize the scaling process without affecting response times?

options.A: Change to a multi-model endpoint configuration in SageMaker AI.
options.B: Integrate Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda to manage invocations of the SageMaker AI inference endpoint.
options.C: Decrease the cooldown period for scale-in activities. Increase the maximum number of instances.
options.D: Increase the cooldown period after scale-out activities.

explanation: SageMaker endpoint auto scaling uses target tracking on InvocationsPerInstance by default. Proper cooldown periods prevent thrashing, and CloudWatch alarms trigger scaling actions.

question: A company deployed an Amazon SageMaker AI ML model to an endpoint by calling the CreateModel API operation. The network that was established with the API call includes two private subnets and one security group. The model must download data from an Amazon S3 bucket and must upload data to the S3 bucket. The traffic to the S3 bucket must not travel across the internet. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create a NAT gateway. Configure the security group to allow outbound connections. Configure route tables to redirect any traffic to the S3 bucket through the NAT gateway.
options.B: Create a gateway VPC endpoint. Configure an endpoint policy that restricts access to the S3 bucket. Configure route tables to redirect any traffic to the S3 bucket through the endpoint.
options.C: Create an interface VPC endpoint. Verify that the security group allows only inbound connections. Configure route tables to redirect any traffic to the S3 bucket through the endpoint.
options.D: Create a Gateway Load Balancer VPC endpoint. Configure an IAM policy that restricts access to the S3 bucket. Configure route tables to redirect any traffic to the S3 bucket through the endpoint.

explanation: SageMaker endpoints reference models via S3 model artifact paths. If the model artifact location or container image URI changes, the endpoint configuration must be updated accordingly.

question: A company is developing a new ML model to rank customers in order of their potential to pay back loans. The company needs to use an Amazon SageMaker AI built-in algorithm. Which algorithm should the company use to meet these requirements?

options.A: XGBoost
options.B: K-means clustering
options.C: Principal component analysis (PCA)
options.D: Neural Topic Model (NTM)

explanation: For ranking tasks (ordering customers by loan repayment probability), the model outputs a continuous score. AUC evaluates ranking quality across all classification thresholds.

question: An ML engineer is setting up an Amazon SageMaker AI pipeline for an ML model. The pipeline must automatically initiate a re-training job if any data drift is detected. How should the ML engineer set up the pipeline to meet this requirement?

options.A: Use an AWS Glue crawler and an AWS Glue extract, transform and load (ETL) job to detect data drift. Use AWS Glue triggers to automate the re-training job.
options.B: Use Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink to detect data drift. Use an AWS Lambda function to automate the re-training job.
options.C: Use SageMaker Model Monitor to detect data drift. Use an AWS Lambda function to automate the re-training job.
options.D: Use Amazon QuickSight anomaly detection to detect data drift. Use an AWS Step Functions workflow to automate the re-training job.

explanation: SageMaker Pipelines QualityCheck step validates data/model quality and automatically registers a baseline in Model Monitor, enabling continuous monitoring after deployment.

question: A company has developed a computer vision model. The company needs to deploy the model into production on Amazon SageMaker AI. The company has not hosted a model on SageMaker AI previously. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to track model versions. The solution also must provide recommendations about which Amazon EC2 instance types to use to host the model. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Register the model in Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR). Use AWS Compute Optimizer for recommendations about instance types.
options.B: Register the model in the SageMaker Model Registry. Use SageMaker Autopilot for recommendations about instance types.
options.C: Register the model in the SageMaker Model Registry. Use SageMaker Inference Recommender for recommendations about instance types.
options.D: Register the model in Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR). Use SageMaker Experiments for recommendations about instance types.

explanation: SageMaker multi-model endpoints (MME) with auto scaling can host thousands of model variants on shared infrastructure, dynamically loading models from S3 as needed.

question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker AI to develop a credit risk assessment model. During model validation, the company finds that the model achieves 82% accuracy on the validation data. However, the model achieved 99% accuracy on the training data. The company needs to address the model accuracy issue before deployment. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Add more dense layers to increase model complexity. Implement batch normalization. Use early stopping during training.
options.B: Implement dropout layers. Use L1 or L2 regularization. Perform k-fold cross-validation.
options.C: Use principal component analysis (PCA) to reduce the feature dimensionality. Decrease model layers. Implement cross-entropy loss functions.
options.D: Augment the training dataset. Remove duplicate records from the training dataset. Implement stratified sampling.

explanation: SageMaker Clarify runs bias analysis using statistical metrics (demographic parity, equalized odds) on training data and predictions, identifying and quantifying unfairness across groups.
