question: A company collects customer data every day. The company stores the data as compressed files in an Amazon S3 bucket that is partitioned by date. Every month, analysts download the data, process the data to check the data quality, and then upload the data to Amazon QuickSight dashboards. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to automatically check the data quality before the data is sent to QuickSight. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Run an AWS Glue crawler every month to update the AWS Glue Data Catalog. Use AWS Glue Data Quality rules to check the data quality.
options.B: Use an AWS Glue trigger to run an AWS Glue crawler every month to update the AWS Glue Data Catalog. Create an AWS Glue job that loads the data into a PySpark DataFrame. Configure the job to apply custom functions and to evaluate the data quality.
options.C: Run Python scripts on an AWS Lambda function every month to evaluate data quality. Configure the S3 bucket to invoke the Lambda function when objects are added to the S3 bucket.
options.D: Configure the S3 bucket to send event notifications to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue when objects are uploaded. Use Amazon CloudWatch insights every month for the SQS queue to evaluate the data quality.

explanation: AWS Glue crawlers automatically infer schemas from compressed files in S3, populating the Glue Data Catalog with table definitions that Athena and SageMaker can query directly.

question: A company has an ML model in Amazon SageMaker AI. An ML engineer needs to implement a monitoring solution to automatically detect changes in the input data distribution of model features. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Configure SageMaker Model Monitor. Establish a data quality baseline. Ensure that the emit_metrics option is enabled in the baseline constraints file. Configure an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to notify the company about changes in specific metrics that are related to data quality.
options.B: Configure SageMaker Model Monitor. Establish a model quality baseline. Ensure that the comparison_method option is set to Robust in the baseline constraints file. Configure an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to notify the company about changes in model quality metrics.
options.C: Use SageMaker Debugger with custom rules to track shifts in feature distributions. Configure Amazon CloudWatch alarms to notify the company when the rules detect significant changes.
options.D: Use Amazon CloudWatch to directly observe the SageMaker AI endpoint's performance metrics. Manually analyze the CloudWatch logs for indicators of data drift or shifts in feature distribution.

explanation: SageMaker Model Monitor with data capture logs inference inputs/outputs, comparing distributions against baselines to detect drift. CloudWatch alarms notify when thresholds are breached.

question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker AI to deploy a new recommendation model for its ecommerce website. The model must use data from all client website interactions as input. Traffic is variable throughout the day. The company needs to create an inference endpoint for the model. Which type of inference endpoint will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

options.A: Batch transform inference endpoint
options.B: Asynchronous inference endpoint
options.C: Real-time inference endpoint
options.D: Serverless inference endpoint

explanation: Blue/green deployment with canary traffic shifting sends a small percentage of traffic to the new model first. If CloudWatch alarms stay healthy, traffic gradually shifts to 100%.

question: A company runs an Amazon SageMaker AI domain in a public subnet of a newly created VPC. The network is configured properly, and ML engineers can access the SageMaker AI domain. Recently, the company discovered suspicious traffic to the domain from a specific IP address. The company needs to block traffic from the specific IP address. Which update to the network configuration will meet this requirement?

options.A: Create a security group inbound rule to deny traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the security group to the domain.
options.B: Create a network ACL inbound rule to deny traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the rule to the default network ACL for the subnet where the domain is located.
options.C: Create a shadow variant for the domain. Configure SageMaker Inference Recommender to send traffic from the specific IP address to the shadow endpoint.
options.D: Create a VPC route table to deny inbound traffic from the specific IP address. Assign the route table to the domain.

explanation: A SageMaker domain in a VPC without NAT or internet gateway cannot reach the public internet. Adding NAT gateway or VPC endpoints for required services restores connectivity.

question: A company's ML engineer is creating a classification model. The ML engineer explores the dataset and notices a column that is named day_of_week. The column's data consists of the following values: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, and Sunday. Which technique should the ML engineer use to convert this column's data to binary values?

options.A: Binary encoding
options.B: Label encoding
options.C: One-hot encoding
options.D: Tokenization

explanation: Features with very low variance (near-zero or constant values) provide no discriminative power for classification. Removing them reduces noise and improves model performance.

question: An ML engineer wants to use Amazon SageMaker AI to prepare data for training. During exploratory data analysis, the ML engineer notices that several categorical features are missing values. How can the ML engineer use SageMaker AI to solve this problem?

options.A: Use SageMaker Clarify to impute categorical features with the mean value.
options.B: Use SageMaker Clarify to impute categorical features with the mode value.
options.C: Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to impute categorical features with the mean value.
options.D: Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to impute categorical features with the mode value.

explanation: SageMaker Data Wrangler provides visual data exploration, profiling, and 300+ built-in transformations within SageMaker Studio — purpose-built for ML data preparation.

question: A company stores user clickstream data in an Amazon S3 bucket in AWS Account A. The company needs to use the data to train an ML model in Amazon SageMaker AI in AWS Account B. The training will take 10 days. The company needs to use only private IP addresses in the training. The company also must make sure that no training metadata is shared with AWS. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Set up VPC peering between Account A and Account B. Contact AWS by email to opt out of metadata collection.
options.B: Set up a VPC endpoint for the S3 bucket. Set the SageMaker AI OPT_OUT_TRACKING environment variable to 1 in the training job.
options.C: Configure a security group policy that is assigned to the S3 bucket in Account A to allow access from only Account B. Create AI services opt-out policies.
options.D: Generate presigned URLs with expiration times for the objects that are stored in the S3 bucket. Access the data by using the presigned URLs. Set the SageMaker AI OPT_OUT_TRACKING environment variable to 1 in the training job.

explanation: Cross-account S3 access requires a bucket policy in Account A granting the SageMaker execution role in Account B s3:GetObject permissions, plus the role having matching IAM permissions.

question: A music streaming company constantly streams song ratings from an application to an Amazon S3 bucket. The company wants to use the ratings as an input for training and inference of an Amazon SageMaker AI model. The company has an AWS Glue Data Catalog that is configured with the S3 bucket as the source. An ML engineer needs to implement a solution to create a repository for this data. The solution must ensure that the data stays synchronized during batch training and real-time inference. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Ingest data into SageMaker Feature Store from the S3 bucket. Apply tags and indexes.
options.B: Use Amazon Athena. Create tables by using CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) queries to group data.
options.C: Use AWS Lake Formation. Apply tag-based control on the data.
options.D: Use the Generate Data Insights function in SageMaker Data Wrangler.

explanation: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink processes Kinesis streams in real time, applying windowed aggregations and transformations before delivering results to downstream systems.

question: A hospital is using an ML model to validate x-ray results. The hospital runs a nightly batch inference job. The hospital needs to produce a daily report about model data quality and model performance. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Schedule a monitoring job in Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor. Generate the monitoring results for the model and data.
options.B: Create an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard that includes the metrics for processing steps in the nightly batch inference job. Compare the baseline resource metrics. Share the dashboard link.
options.C: Use AWS Glue DataBrew to create a custom recipe job that uses the Numerical Statistics data quality check for the model file. Generate the results.
options.D: Create a SageMaker AI pipeline that includes a QualityCheck step to run monitoring jobs. Generate the monitoring results for the model and the data.

explanation: SageMaker Batch Transform processes large datasets asynchronously without a persistent endpoint. Scheduling via EventBridge + Lambda provides a cost-effective nightly inference solution.

question: A company needs to ingest data from data sources into Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler. The data sources are Amazon S3, Amazon Redshift, and Snowflake. The ingested data must always be up to date with the latest changes in the source systems. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use direct connections to import data from the data sources into Data Wrangler.
options.B: Use cataloged connections to import data from the data sources into Data Wrangler.
options.C: Use AWS Glue to extract data from the data sources. Use AWS Glue also to import the data directly into Data Wrangler.
options.D: Use AWS Lambda to extract data from the data sources. Use Lambda also to import the data directly into Data Wrangler.

explanation: SageMaker Data Wrangler supports direct connections to Athena, S3, Redshift, Snowflake, and Databricks — enabling data import from diverse sources without manual export/upload.

question: An ML engineer is using Amazon SageMaker Canvas to build a custom ML model from an imported dataset. The ML engineer wants the model to make continuous numeric predictions based on 10 years of data. Which metric should the ML engineer use to evaluate the model's performance?

options.A: Accuracy
options.B: InferenceLatency
options.C: Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC)
options.D: Root mean square error (RMSE)

explanation: When the target variable is time-series data (e.g., monthly revenue), SageMaker Canvas's time-series forecasting model type with a configurable forecast horizon is the appropriate choice.

question: A company has built, trained, and tuned two new ML models: Model A detects if a transaction is fraudulent based on the IP address, location, and user credentials. This model will be accessed every time a transaction occurs. Model B forecasts sales totals for the next month based on historical sales data. This model will be accessed one time each month. The company must deploy both models to production by using Amazon SageMaker AI. Which hosting solution for the models should the company use to meet these requirements?

options.A: Host both models in one container behind one real-time endpoint.
options.B: Host Model A with an asynchronous endpoint. Host Model B with a real-time endpoint.
options.C: Host Model A with a real-time endpoint. Use batch transform for Model B.
options.D: Use batch transform for Model A. Host Model B with an asynchronous endpoint.

explanation: SageMaker Inference Pipeline chains multiple containers sequentially in a single endpoint, enabling multi-stage inference (e.g., fraud detection → risk scoring) without separate endpoints.

question: A bank needs to use Amazon SageMaker AI to create an ML model to determine which customers qualify for a new product. The bank must use algorithms that SageMaker AI directly supports. The model must be explainable to the bank's regulators. Which modeling approach will meet these requirements?

options.A: Train the model by using the Object2Vec algorithm.
options.B: Train the model by using the linear learner algorithm.
options.C: Train a neural network.
options.D: Train the model by using the k-means algorithm.

explanation: SageMaker Clarify produces bias reports and SHAP-based explainability reports, helping regulated industries (banking, healthcare) demonstrate model fairness and decision transparency.

question: An ML engineer is building a model to predict house and apartment prices. The model uses three features: Square Meters, Price, and Age of Building. The dataset has 10,000 data rows. The data includes data points for one large mansion and one extremely small apartment. The ML engineer must perform preprocessing on the dataset to ensure that the model produces accurate predictions for the typical house or apartment. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Remove the outliers and perform a log transformation on the Square Meters variable.
options.B: Keep the outliers and perform normalization on the Square Meters variable.
options.C: Remove the outliers and perform one-hot encoding on the Square Meters variable.
options.D: Keep the outliers and perform one-hot encoding on the Square Meters variable.

explanation: Log-transforming right-skewed target variables (like house prices) normalizes the distribution. Feature splitting extracts components (year, month) from dates for temporal patterns.

question: A company is developing an ML model by using Amazon SageMaker AI. The company must monitor bias in the model and must display the results on a dashboard. An ML engineer creates a bias monitoring job. How should the ML engineer capture bias metrics to display on the dashboard?

options.A: Capture AWS CloudTrail metrics from SageMaker Clarify.
options.B: Capture Amazon CloudWatch metrics from SageMaker Clarify.
options.C: Capture SageMaker Model Monitor metrics from Amazon EventBridge.
options.D: Capture SageMaker Model Monitor metrics from Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS).

explanation: SageMaker Clarify scheduled via Model Monitor checks for bias drift in production predictions over time, comparing against a baseline and alerting when bias metrics exceed thresholds.

question: A company is using an Amazon SageMaker AI ML model to predict traffic accidents that potholes cause. An ML engineer has configured SageMaker Model Monitor to run as part of a SageMaker AI pipeline. In the MonitoringExecution output, the ML engineer observes several baseline_drift_check violations that are failing the pipeline. What should the ML engineer do to resolve this issue?

options.A: Retrain the model by using a new SageMaker AI training job. Check for errors by using SageMaker Debugger.
options.B: Retrain the model with new training data. Reuse the original baseline in Model Monitor.
options.C: Retrain the model with new training data. Use the new baseline in Model Monitor.
options.D: Rerun the SageMaker AI pipeline after enabling the emit_metrics option in the baseline constraints file.

explanation: Increasing training data size helps the model generalize better. Increasing epochs with regularization (dropout, weight decay) also improves robustness for safety-critical applications.

question: A company uses ML models to predict whether transactions are fraudulent. The company needs to identify as many fraudulent transactions as possible. Which evaluation metric should the company use to evaluate the models to meet this requirement?

options.A: F1 score
options.B: Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC)
options.C: Precision
options.D: Recall

explanation: SageMaker Clarify's SHAP-based feature attributions show which features most influenced each fraud prediction, providing interpretable explanations for compliance and investigation teams.

question: An ML engineer has trained an ML model by using Amazon SageMaker AI. The ML engineer determines that the model is overfitting and that the training data contains unnecessary features. The ML engineer must reduce the overfitting and the impact of the unnecessary features. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Increase the number of training iterations. Retrain the model.
options.B: Apply L1 regularization to the training data. Retrain the model.
options.C: Decrease the number of training iterations. Retrain the model.
options.D: Use SageMaker Debugger to apply L1 regularization to the running model.

explanation: SageMaker Automatic Model Tuning (AMT) runs multiple training jobs with different hyperparameter values, using Bayesian optimization or random search to find the optimal configuration.

question: An ML engineer wants to use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to perform preprocessing on a dataset. The ML engineer wants to use the processed dataset to train a classification model. During preprocessing, the ML engineer notices that a text feature has a range of thousands of values that differ only by spelling errors. The ML engineer needs to apply an encoding method so that after preprocessing is complete, the text feature can be used to train the model. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Perform ordinal encoding to represent categories of the feature.
options.B: Perform similarity encoding to represent categories of the feature.
options.C: Perform one-hot encoding to represent categories of the feature.
options.D: Perform target encoding to represent categories of the feature.

explanation: SageMaker Data Wrangler recipes export directly as SageMaker Processing job code or Pipelines steps, enabling seamless integration of data transformations into automated ML workflows.

question: An ML engineer is training a text generation model on Amazon SageMaker AI. After several epochs, the loss function does not converge, and the model's accuracy on the validation dataset starts to show oscillating results. The ML engineer needs to ensure that the model achieves generalization. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Increase the learning rate and decrease the mini-batch size.
options.B: Increase the learning rate as the number of epochs increases.
options.C: Decrease the learning rate and increase the mini-batch size.
options.D: Decrease the learning rate and decrease the mini-batch size.

explanation: When training loss decreases but validation loss increases, the model is overfitting. Regularization techniques (dropout, weight decay, early stopping) constrain complexity and improve generalization.

question: A company uses an NFS-based data store to store data for ML training. Linux-based systems access the data store. The company needs a hybrid system to make the shared data store accessible to on-premises servers and Amazon SageMaker AI notebooks that will consume the data. File locking is required for the data producers. Which AWS storage solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use an Amazon S3 bucket to store the data. Use Mountpoint for Amazon S3 to mount the S3 bucket to the on-premises servers and the SageMaker AI notebooks.
options.B: Use an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system to store the data. Mount the file system to the on-premises servers and the SageMaker AI notebooks.
options.C: Use an Amazon FSx for Lustre file system to store the data. Mount the file system to the on-premises servers and the SageMaker AI notebooks.
options.D: Use an Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume to store the data. Mount the volume to the on-premises servers and the SageMaker AI notebooks.

explanation: Amazon FSx for Lustre provides a POSIX-compliant, high-throughput parallel file system that integrates natively with S3, making it ideal for Linux-based ML training on large datasets.

question: A company needs to analyze a large dataset that is stored in Amazon S3 in Apache Parquet format. The company wants to use one-hot encoding for some of the columns. The company needs a no-code solution to transform the data. The solution must store the transformed data back to the same S3 bucket for model training. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Configure an AWS Glue DataBrew project that connects to the data. Use the DataBrew interactive interface to create a recipe that performs the one-hot encoding transformation. Create a job to apply the transformation and to write the output back to an S3 bucket.
options.B: Configure an AWS Glue Data Catalog table that points to the data. Use Amazon Athena to write SQL commands to perform the one-hot encoding transformation. Configure Athena to write the query results back to an S3 bucket.
options.C: Configure an AWS Glue Data Catalog table that points to the data. Create an AWS Glue ETL interactive notebook. Use the notebook to perform the one-hot encoding transformation. Run the configured cells and write the results back to an S3 bucket.
options.D: Configure an Amazon Redshift cluster to access the data by using Redshift Spectrum. Use SQL commands to perform the one-hot encoding transformation within Amazon Redshift. Configure Amazon Redshift to write the results back to an S3 bucket.

explanation: Amazon Athena queries Parquet files in S3 directly using serverless SQL, with automatic partition pruning and columnar read optimization — no infrastructure to manage.

question: A company wants to migrate ML models from an on-premises environment to Amazon SageMaker AI. The models are based on the PyTorch algorithm. The company needs to reuse its existing custom scripts as much as possible on AWS. Which feature of SageMaker AI should the company use to meet these requirements?

options.A: SageMaker AI built-in algorithms
options.B: SageMaker Canvas
options.C: SageMaker JumpStart
options.D: SageMaker AI script mode

explanation: SageMaker supports BYOC (Bring Your Own Container) via ECR, allowing any custom framework or algorithm to be packaged in a Docker container and run on SageMaker managed infrastructure.

question: A company uses an Amazon QuickSight dashboard to track the sale prices of sneakers over time. The dashboard aggregates sale prices scraped from many retail websites. The company wants to determine which prices are unusually high outliers and to display the outliers visually. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use a vertical bar chart to visualize the outliers. Use a calculated field in QuickSight to take the square roots of the outlier prices to generate the chart. Configure a custom AWS Lambda function to scan the data for anomalies.
options.B: Use AWS Glue DataBrew to preprocess the data. Set the REMOVE_OUTLIERS operation to eliminate data rows that include unusually high prices. Invoke an AWS Lambda function to store the removed rows in Amazon DynamoDB.
options.C: Use a vertical bar chart to visualize the outliers. Use a calculated field in QuickSight to square the outlier prices to generate the chart. Use QuickSight anomaly detection insights to determine which prices are unusually high.
options.D: Use a QuickSight filter to find the lowest 10 values for sneaker price. Assign a specific color to the 10 lowest values.

explanation: Box plots display median, quartiles, and outliers, making them ideal for visualizing price distributions across categories and identifying anomalous data points in time-series data.

question: An ML engineer is using Amazon QuickSight anomaly detection to detect very high or very low machine operating temperatures compared to normal. The ML engineer sets the Severity parameter to Low and above. The ML engineer sets the Direction parameter to All. What effect will the ML engineer observe in the anomaly detection results if the ML engineer changes the Direction parameter to Lower than expected?

options.A: Increased anomaly identification frequency and increased recall
options.B: Decreased anomaly identification frequency and decreased recall
options.C: Increased anomaly identification frequency and decreased recall
options.D: Decreased anomaly identification frequency and increased recall

explanation: Amazon QuickSight ML-powered anomaly detection uses Random Cut Forest (RCF) to identify unusual patterns in time-series data, with configurable sensitivity and automatic alerting.

question: A company runs its ML workflows on an on-premises Kubernetes cluster. The ML workflows include ML services that perform training and inferences for ML models. Each ML service runs from its own standalone Docker image. The company needs to perform a lift and shift from the on-premises Kubernetes cluster to an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (Amazon EKS) cluster. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Redesign the ML services to be configured in Kubeflow. Deploy the new Kubeflow managed ML services to the EKS cluster.
options.B: Upload the Docker images to an Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) repository. Configure a deployment pipeline to deploy the images to the EKS cluster.
options.C: Migrate the training data to an Amazon Redshift cluster. Retrain the models from the migrated training data by using Amazon Redshift ML. Deploy the retrained models to the EKS cluster.
options.D: Configure an Amazon SageMaker AI notebook. Retrain the models with the same code. Deploy the retrained models to the EKS cluster.

explanation: Amazon SageMaker Operators for Kubernetes enable running SageMaker training and inference jobs from existing Kubernetes clusters, providing a bridge for on-premises K8s migration to AWS.

question: A retail company is creating an AI-powered assistant for customers. The company has a large body of documentation that the assistant needs to use for general inquiries. The company wants any responses about prices to use only documentation that is less than 1 month old. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use Amazon Q Business to develop the responses. Configure a document attribute filter so that responses about prices use only the documents from the past month.
options.B: Use Amazon Q Business to develop the responses. Configure the source attribution citation so that responses about prices use only the documents from the past month.
options.C: Segment the documents into folders based on the month of document creation. Configure Amazon Q Developer to use only the documents from the past month to develop responses about prices.
options.D: Segment the documents into folders based on the month of document creation. Grant the assistant access to only the documents from the past month for responses about prices. Use Amazon Q Developer to develop the responses.

explanation: Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases with RAG retrieves relevant company documents and grounds the LLM's responses in factual content, reducing hallucination for customer-facing AI assistants.

question: A travel company wants to create an ML model to recommend the next airport destination for its users. The company has collected millions of data records about user location, recent search history on the company's website, and 2,000 available airports. The data has several categorical features with a target column that is expected to have a high-dimensional sparse matrix. The company needs to use Amazon SageMaker AI built-in algorithms for the model. An ML engineer converts the categorical features by using one-hot encoding. Which algorithm should the ML engineer implement to meet these requirements?

options.A: Use the CatBoost algorithm to recommend the next airport destination.
options.B: Use the DeepAR forecasting algorithm to recommend the next airport destination.
options.C: Use the Factorization Machines algorithm to recommend the next airport destination.
options.D: Use the k-means algorithm to cluster users into groups. Map each group to the next airport destination based on user search history.

explanation: Collaborative filtering recommends items based on similar users' behavior patterns. User-based CF identifies users with similar travel histories to suggest destinations they haven't visited.

question: An ML engineer is configuring auto scaling for an inference component of a model that runs behind an Amazon SageMaker AI endpoint. The ML engineer configures SageMaker AI auto scaling with a target tracking scaling policy set to 100 invocations per model per minute. The SageMaker AI endpoint scales appropriately during normal business hours. However, the ML engineer notices that at the start of each business day, there are zero instances available to handle requests, which causes delays in processing. The ML engineer must ensure that the SageMaker AI endpoint can handle incoming requests at the start of each business day. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Reduce the SageMaker AI auto scaling cooldown period to the minimum supported value. Add an auto scaling lifecycle hook to scale the SageMaker AI instances.
options.B: Change the target metric to CPU utilization.
options.C: Modify the scaling policy target value to one.
options.D: Apply a step scaling policy that scales based on an Amazon CloudWatch alarm. Apply a second CloudWatch alarm and scaling policy to scale the minimum number of instances from zero to one at the start of each business day.

explanation: SageMaker inference component auto scaling uses target tracking on specified metrics. Setting MinCopyCount and MaxCopyCount defines the scaling boundaries for the inference component.

question: An ML engineer is using an Amazon SageMaker Studio notebook to train a neural network by creating an estimator. The estimator runs a Python training script that uses Distributed Data Parallel (DDP) on a single instance that has more than one GPU. The ML engineer discovers that the training script is underutilizing GPU resources. The ML engineer must identify the point in the training script where resource utilization can be optimized. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Use Amazon CloudWatch metrics to create a report that describes GPU utilization over time.
options.B: Add SageMaker Profiler annotations to the training script. Run the script and generate a report from the results.
options.C: Use AWS CloudTrail to create a report that describes GPU utilization and GPU memory utilization over time.
options.D: Create a default monitor in Amazon SageMaker Model Monitor and suggest a baseline. Generate a report based on the constraints and statistics the monitor generates.

explanation: Checkpointing saves model state periodically during training. If a Spot Instance is interrupted, training resumes from the last checkpoint instead of restarting from scratch.

question: A company is developing an internal cost-estimation tool that uses an ML model in Amazon SageMaker AI. Users upload high-resolution images to the tool. The model must process each image and predict the cost of the object in the image. The model also must notify the user when processing is complete. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Store the images in an Amazon S3 bucket. Deploy the model on SageMaker AI. Use batch transform jobs for model inference. Use an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue to notify users.
options.B: Store the images in an Amazon S3 bucket. Deploy the model on SageMaker AI. Use an asynchronous inference strategy for model inference. Use an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to notify users.
options.C: Store the images in an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system. Deploy the model on SageMaker AI. Use batch transform jobs for model inference. Use an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue to notify users.
options.D: Store the images in an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system. Deploy the model on SageMaker AI. Use an asynchronous inference strategy for model inference. Use an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to notify users.

explanation: SageMaker Model Monitor with a custom baseline tailored to the company's data distribution detects cost-estimation accuracy degradation by comparing predictions against actual values.

question: A healthcare company uses an Amazon SageMaker AI endpoint to host a model that predicts patient readmission risk to hospitals. The company wants to predict patient readmissions with high accuracy and is willing to tolerate false positives. The current model performance has degraded over the previous year. The company trains and deploys a new model as a shadow variant for testing on live traffic from hospitals. The company monitors the performance of the new model for a month. During the month of testing, the shadow variant has a higher recall than the existing model but has a lower precision. What should the company do next?

options.A: Promote the shadow variant to full production.
options.B: Extend the shadow testing period to capture more data. Monitor the new model to determine whether precision improves.
options.C: Use a blue/green deployment strategy to allocate a small percentage of traffic to the shadow variant to reduce model errors.
options.D: Disable the shadow variant and roll back to the main variant.

explanation: SageMaker Model Monitor's model quality monitoring compares endpoint predictions against ground truth labels, tracking accuracy/F1/RMSE over time and alerting on degradation.

question: An airline company uses an ML model to adjust ticket prices based on demand. The model runs on Amazon SageMaker real-time endpoints. During previous deployments, the model failed to scale quickly enough when website traffic increased, which caused delays in price adjustments. An ML engineer needs to configure auto scaling for the SageMaker endpoints to respond rapidly to traffic changes. The solution must use target tracking scaling policies. Which configuration will be MOST responsive to sudden changes in traffic?

options.A: Configure auto scaling based on the SageMaker AI InvocationsPerInstance standard metric. Configure 10-second interval resolution, and set the default 300-second scale-in cooldown period.
options.B: Configure auto scaling based on the SageMaker AI InvocationsPerInstance metric. Configure high-resolution 10-second intervals, and set a 600-second scale-in cooldown period.
options.C: Configure auto scaling based on the SageMaker InvocationsPerInstance standard metric. Configure 10-second intervals resolution, and set a 600-second scale-in cooldown period.
options.D: Configure auto scaling based on the SageMaker InvocationsPerInstance metric. Configure high-resolution 10-second intervals, and set the default 300-second scale-in cooldown period.

explanation: SageMaker deployment guardrails with automatic rollback use CloudWatch alarms monitoring prediction quality. If the new model's metrics breach alarm thresholds, traffic reverts to the previous version.

question: An ML engineer is collecting data to train a classification ML model by using Amazon SageMaker AI. The target column can have two possible values: Class A or Class B. The ML engineer wants to ensure that the number of samples for both Class A and Class B are balanced, without losing any existing training data. The ML engineer must test the balance of the training data. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Use SageMaker Clarify to check for class imbalance (CI). If the value is equal to 0, then use random undersampling in SageMaker Data Wrangler to balance the classes.
options.B: Use SageMaker Clarify to check for class imbalance (CI). If the value is greater than 0, then use synthetic minority oversampling technique (SMOTE) in SageMaker Data Wrangler to balance the classes.
options.C: Use SageMaker JumpStart to generate a class imbalance (CI) report. If the value is greater than 0, then use random undersampling in SageMaker Studio to balance the classes.
options.D: Use SageMaker JumpStart to generate a class imbalance (CI) report. If the value is equal to 0, then use synthetic minority oversampling technique (SMOTE) in SageMaker Studio to balance the classes.

explanation: Stratified sampling ensures all classes are proportionally represented in training, validation, and test splits — critical for classification models with imbalanced class distributions.

question: An ML engineer is building a logistic regression model to predict customer churn for subscription services. The ML engineer is using a dataset that contains two string variables: location and job_seniority_level. The location variable has 3 distinct values, and the job_seniority_level variable has over 10 distinct values. The ML engineer must perform preprocessing on the variables. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Apply tokenization to location. Apply ordinal encoding to job_seniority_level.
options.B: Apply one-hot encoding to location. Apply ordinal encoding to job_seniority_level.
options.C: Apply binning to location. Apply standard scaling to job_seniority_level.
options.D: Apply one-hot encoding to location. Apply standard scaling to job_seniority_level.

explanation: L1 regularization (Lasso) drives less-important feature weights to exactly zero, performing automatic feature selection and reducing overfitting in logistic regression models.

question: A company needs to deploy a custom-trained classification ML model on AWS. The model must make near real-time predictions with low latency and must handle variable request volumes. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create an Amazon SageMaker AI batch transform job to process inference requests in batches.
options.B: Use Amazon API Gateway to receive prediction requests. Use an Amazon S3 bucket to host and serve the model.
options.C: Deploy an Amazon SageMaker AI endpoint. Configure auto scaling for the endpoint.
options.D: Launch AWS Deep Learning AMIs (DLAMI) on two Amazon EC2 instances. Run the instances behind an Application Load Balancer.

explanation: SageMaker real-time endpoints provide synchronous, low-latency inference with auto scaling. Custom containers via ECR support any model framework for production deployment.

question: A company runs a neural network model and retrains the model when the performance degrades. The company uses a training job that uses Amazon SageMaker AI distributed data parallelism (DDP). The training job takes several hours to run. The company wants to decrease the required time for the training job. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Increase the number of epochs.
options.B: Increase the number of neurons in the hidden layers.
options.C: Increase the number of layers.
options.D: Increase the number of instances.

explanation: SageMaker Automatic Model Tuning with warm-start reuses results from previous tuning jobs, exploring the hyperparameter space more efficiently and converging faster on subsequent runs.

question: An ML engineer needs to build a processing pipeline to identify and remove personally identifiable information (PII) from petabytes of unstructured data. The ML engineer will use the processed data to train ML models in Amazon SageMaker AI. Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Use the Apache Spark-based serverless engine from AWS Glue interactive sessions. Use the Detect PII transform feature to identify and remove the PII data.
options.B: Use AWS Glue Data Wrangler within Amazon SageMaker Canvas to detect and remove the PII.
options.C: Use the Amazon SageMaker Clarify API to detect and mask the PII data.
options.D: Use the DetectEntities API action in Amazon Comprehend to identify and remove the PII data.

explanation: Amazon Comprehend detects PII entities in text (names, SSNs, addresses). A processing pipeline using Comprehend can identify, flag, and redact PII before storing data in the data lake.

question: A company is using Amazon SageMaker AI to create a classification model to categorize the company's sales performance for each month of the previous 20 years on a scale from 1 to 5. The dataset includes fields for month, sales region, regional aggregate sales, and the number of stores in each sales region. The company notices that during two months of every year, the aggregate sales values are unexpectedly high. The company performs one-hot encoding on all non-numerical features in the training and validation datasets. The company uses the training dataset to train the classification model. When the company evaluates the model against the validation dataset, the results are less accurate than expected. The company must improve the model's accuracy on the validation dataset. Which solution will meet this requirement?

options.A: Remove records that include outliers across all features.
options.B: Use a stratified split on the month and sales region features.
options.C: Perform normalization on the aggregate sales feature.
options.D: Perform normalization on the aggregate sales feature for each sales region.

explanation: Stratified splitting on month and sales region ensures both training and validation sets contain representative samples from each time period and region, improving model generalization.

question: A data scientist needs a highly available, scalable network file system that supports features like snapshots, cloning, and automated tiering for a large ML dataset. Which AWS storage service best meets these requirements?

options.A: Amazon S3 with Lifecycle Policies
options.B: Amazon EFS
options.C: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP
options.D: Amazon FSx for Lustre

explanation: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP provides enterprise NAS features including snapshots, cloning, and automated tiering between SSD and capacity storage — unique among AWS file services.

question: An ML pipeline requires ingesting streaming data into Amazon S3 with minimal setup and overhead. Which AWS service provides a fully managed way to do this, handling automatic scaling, buffering, and optional compression/encryption?

options.A: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
options.B: Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose (now Amazon Data Firehose)
options.C: Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka (MSK)
options.D: AWS DataSync

explanation: Amazon Data Firehose (formerly Kinesis Data Firehose) auto-scales to match data throughput and delivers to S3 with optional batching, compression, and encryption — fully managed, zero administration.

question: A team is building ML models that reuse many features (e.g. customer age, account status) across multiple projects. They want a central, managed repository to store, share, and retrieve these features for both training (batch) and online inference. Which AWS service should they use?

options.A: Amazon DynamoDB
options.B: AWS Glue Data Catalog
options.C: Amazon SageMaker Feature Store
options.D: Amazon Redshift

explanation: SageMaker Feature Store provides a centralized repository with both online (low-latency) and offline (batch) stores, ensuring consistent feature values across training and inference.

question: A company needs to create a labeled image dataset by having humans label millions of images (e.g. object bounding boxes). Which AWS service provides built-in workflows and workforce options (including Mechanical Turk) to efficiently label data for ML?

options.A: Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth
options.B: AWS Step Functions
options.C: Amazon Mechanical Turk (MTurk) only
options.D: AWS DataBrew

explanation: SageMaker Ground Truth provides built-in labeling workflows (bounding box, segmentation, classification) with workforce options including Mechanical Turk, vendors, and private teams.

question: A data engineer wants to clean and normalize a large dataset with a visual, no-code interface, applying many predefined transformations (filter anomalies, format conversion, etc.). Which AWS service is best suited for this task?

options.A: Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler
options.B: AWS Glue DataBrew
options.C: Amazon EMR with Hadoop
options.D: Amazon SageMaker Processing

explanation: AWS Glue DataBrew offers 250+ pre-built visual transformations for data cleaning and normalization without writing code — designed for data analysts and engineers who prefer no-code interfaces.

question: A startup wants to add real-time image recognition of celebrities to its app but has very limited ML expertise. Which AWS service should they use to quickly identify celebrities in images?

options.A: Amazon Rekognition
options.B: Amazon SageMaker Training with a deep learning model
options.C: Amazon Textract
options.D: AWS Lambda with custom code

explanation: Amazon Rekognition provides pre-trained APIs for celebrity recognition, face detection, and object analysis — enabling image recognition features without any ML expertise.

question: A machine learning engineer runs frequent training jobs with similar configurations during iterative model tuning. They want to reduce the startup latency for each new job so that provisioned instances can be reused. Which SageMaker feature should they use?

options.A: Multi-model endpoints
options.B: SageMaker Training with Managed Spot Instances
options.C: SageMaker Managed Warm Pools
options.D: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling Groups

explanation: SageMaker managed warm pools retain provisioned instances between training jobs, eliminating the provisioning overhead that normally adds minutes to each job startup.

question: A neural network is overfitting the training data (very low training loss but high validation loss). Which technique is most appropriate to reduce overfitting in the model?

options.A: Increase the learning rate
options.B: Add dropout layers or L2 regularization
options.C: Remove batch normalization
options.D: Use a smaller training dataset

explanation: Dropout randomly disables neurons during training, forcing the network to learn redundant representations. L2 regularization penalizes large weights. Both reduce overfitting.

question: A data scientist wants to automatically find the best hyperparameters for their SageMaker training job (e.g. number of layers, learning rate) without manually tuning. Which SageMaker feature should they use?

options.A: SageMaker Automatic Model Tuning (Hyperparameter Tuning Jobs)
options.B: SageMaker Model Monitor
options.C: AWS Batch
options.D: SageMaker Pipelines

explanation: SageMaker Automatic Model Tuning (AMT) supports Bayesian, random, grid, and Hyperband strategies to systematically search hyperparameter spaces and optimize model performance.

question: A company needs to deploy a trained deep learning model for inference on edge devices (e.g., mobile and IoT) with low latency. Which AWS service should they use to optimize and package the model for such devices?

options.A: AWS IoT Greengrass
options.B: Amazon SageMaker Neo
options.C: Amazon EC2 GPU instances
options.D: AWS Lambda

explanation: SageMaker Neo compiles trained models for specific hardware targets (ARM, x86, GPU), optimizing execution speed and memory footprint for edge and mobile deployment.

question: An ML team wants to implement a CI/CD pipeline for their model training and deployment workflows using AWS developer tools. Which combination of AWS services would be most appropriate for version control and automated deployment?

options.A: AWS CodePipeline with AWS CodeCommit/CodeBuild/CodeDeploy
options.B: Amazon SageMaker Processing and Amazon QuickSight
options.C: AWS Step Functions and Amazon CloudWatch Events
options.D: AWS DataPipeline and AWS Batch

explanation: AWS CodePipeline orchestrates source → build → test → deploy stages. CodeCommit stores code, CodeBuild runs tests/training, and CodeDeploy handles deployment — a complete CI/CD solution.

question: A deployed SageMaker endpoint is experiencing increased traffic. To handle this, which feature should be configured to automatically scale the number of inference instances based on utilization?

options.A: SageMaker Endpoint Auto Scaling with target tracking (e.g., CPU utilization)
options.B: AWS Lambda concurrency settings
options.C: Amazon CloudFront with caching
options.D: AWS Elastic Load Balancer (ELB)

explanation: SageMaker endpoint auto scaling uses target tracking policies (e.g., InvocationsPerInstance or CPUUtilization) to automatically add or remove instances based on demand.

question: After deploying a model to production, you want to automatically detect when incoming data distributions change significantly (data drift). Which Amazon SageMaker feature is designed for monitoring such data and model quality in real time?

options.A: SageMaker Model Monitor
options.B: Amazon CloudWatch Logs
options.C: SageMaker Clarify (after training)
options.D: Amazon Inspector

explanation: SageMaker Model Monitor continuously compares production data distributions against a baseline, detecting data drift, model quality degradation, and bias changes in deployed models.

question: A company wants to reduce costs on SageMaker training and inference. Which of the following AWS purchasing options can significantly lower compute costs if the workload is flexible and can tolerate interruptions?

options.A: AWS Reserved Instances and SageMaker Savings Plans
options.B: Provisioned Instances only
options.C: AWS Spot Instances
options.D: Pre-paid monthly flat-rate SageMaker license

explanation: Spot Instances offer up to 90% savings over On-Demand pricing for interruptible workloads. ML training jobs with checkpointing can tolerate Spot interruptions and resume training.

question: What is the best practice to secure access to your SageMaker resources (models, data) and ensure that only authorized entities can access them?

options.A: Configure IAM roles and policies with the principle of least privilege for all ML resources.
options.B: Always use root AWS credentials for SageMaker operations.
options.C: Disable Amazon VPC and security groups so the model is public.
options.D: Store all model artifacts in an unencrypted S3 bucket.

explanation: IAM roles and policies with least-privilege access ensure only authorized users and services can access SageMaker resources, S3 data, and KMS keys — the foundation of AWS ML security.

question: A company stores historical data in .csv files in Amazon S3. Only some of the rows and columns in the .csv files are populated. The columns are not labeled. An ML engineer must prepare and store the data for ML training. Which combination of steps should the ML engineer take to prepare the data with the LEAST operational overhead?

options.A: Use Amazon SageMaker batch transform for data cleaning, then store results in S3.
options.B: Use AWS Glue crawlers to infer schemas, then use AWS Glue DataBrew for data cleaning and feature engineering, then store the resulting data in Amazon S3.
options.C: Use SageMaker Data Wrangler to visualize the data, then use SageMaker Processing for cleaning, then store in Amazon Redshift.
options.D: Manually inspect the CSV files in a notebook, label columns by hand, and upload cleaned files to S3.

explanation: Glue crawlers auto-discover schemas from unstructured data; DataBrew provides 250+ no-code transformations for cleaning. Together they prepare raw CSV data for ML with minimal effort.

question: An ML engineer is preparing a regression model for home prices. The features include Price, Date Sold, Neighborhood (categorical), Square Footage, and Latitude/Longitude. Which set of preprocessing techniques is MOST appropriate for Price, Date Sold, and Neighborhood respectively?

options.A: Price: Logarithmic Transformation, Date Sold: Feature Splitting (year/month), Neighborhood: One-hot Encoding
options.B: Price: Standardization, Date Sold: One-hot Encoding, Neighborhood: Logarithmic Transformation
options.C: Price: Min-Max Scaling, Date Sold: Label Encoding, Neighborhood: Normalization
options.D: Price: One-hot Encoding, Date Sold: Logarithmic Transformation, Neighborhood: Feature Splitting

explanation: Log-transform normalizes right-skewed prices; feature-splitting extracts temporal patterns from dates; one-hot encoding converts nominal categories to binary vectors — standard preprocessing.

question: An ML engineer must unify transaction logs and customer profiles stored in Amazon S3 with on-premises MySQL data. The engineer needs a service that supports ingestion, cataloging, and integration from both cloud and on-premises sources with minimal operational overhead. Which AWS service is BEST suited?

options.A: Amazon EMR Spark jobs
options.B: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
options.C: Amazon DynamoDB
options.D: AWS Lake Formation

explanation: AWS Lake Formation with Glue connectors provides a unified data lake for cloud (S3) and on-premises (JDBC) sources, handling ingestion, cataloging, and access control centrally.

question: An ML engineer is preprocessing data that contains many missing values and inconsistent formats before SageMaker training. The engineer needs a scalable, managed solution. What is the best practice?

options.A: Drop all rows with any missing values to ensure clean data.
options.B: Use AWS Glue jobs to clean, transform, and standardize the data at scale.
options.C: Train the model with the raw data and let SageMaker handle missing values automatically.
options.D: Use Amazon Athena SQL queries to fix formatting issues in-place in S3.

explanation: AWS Glue ETL jobs provide serverless, managed data transformation at scale, handling missing values, format standardization, and schema mapping across large datasets.

question: A fraud detection dataset has severe class imbalance (99.5% legitimate, 0.5% fraud). An ML engineer needs to balance the dataset with the LEAST operational effort. Which solution should the engineer use?

options.A: Write a custom PySpark job on Amazon EMR to implement SMOTE.
options.B: Use SageMaker Studio Classic built-in sampling functions.
options.C: Use AWS Glue DataBrew built-in features to oversample the minority class.
options.D: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler's balance data operation to oversample the minority class.

explanation: SageMaker Data Wrangler's balance data operation applies SMOTE or random oversampling via a visual interface — the fastest way to address class imbalance without writing code.

question: An ecommerce application generates high-volume clickstream data. The data must be ingested, processed, and visualized in near real time. The solution must support SQL for data processing and Jupyter notebooks for interactive analysis. Which architecture meets these requirements?

options.A: Amazon Data Firehose → AWS Lambda → Amazon S3 → Amazon QuickSight
options.B: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams → Amazon Data Firehose → Amazon Athena → Amazon QuickSight
options.C: Amazon MSK → AWS Glue with PySpark → Amazon S3 → Amazon QuickSight
options.D: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams → Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink (Studio Notebooks) → Amazon OpenSearch Service

explanation: Amazon Managed Flink Studio Notebooks support both streaming SQL and interactive Jupyter analysis on Kinesis streams — uniquely combining real-time processing with exploratory analytics.

question: An ML engineer needs to build data ingestion pipelines and ML model deployment pipelines. Raw data is stored in Amazon S3 in various formats. Which combination provides the BEST approach for batch ETL and ML orchestration?

options.A: Amazon Data Firehose → SageMaker Studio Classic
options.B: AWS Glue → SageMaker Studio Classic
options.C: Amazon Redshift ML → SageMaker Studio Classic
options.D: Amazon Athena → SageMaker notebook instances

explanation: AWS Glue provides serverless batch ETL for S3 data; SageMaker Studio provides the ML IDE for training and deployment — together they form a complete data-to-model pipeline.

question: An ML engineer must query thousands of CSV objects in a central Amazon S3 bucket, filtered by transaction date, with the LEAST operational overhead. Which approach should the engineer use?

options.A: Use Amazon Athena with CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) to query and store results based on transaction date.
options.B: Use AWS Glue ETL jobs to load data into Amazon Redshift Spectrum for querying.
options.C: Use AWS Glue Spark jobs to query and store filtered results in a new S3 bucket.
options.D: Use Amazon Data Firehose with AWS Lambda to filter and partition the data.

explanation: Athena CTAS queries S3 directly with serverless SQL, outputting filtered/partitioned results back to S3 — the lowest-overhead approach for querying and restructuring CSV data.

question: An ML engineer must use Amazon SageMaker Canvas with complex-structured data stored in Amazon S3. The engineer wants to minimize processing time when loading data. Which file format should the engineer use?

options.A: CSV compressed with Snappy
options.B: JSONL (JSON Lines)
options.C: Apache Parquet
options.D: TFRecord

explanation: Apache Parquet's columnar format with built-in compression provides faster reads and smaller storage than CSV or JSON, making it the optimal format for SageMaker Canvas data loading.

question: In machine learning, the process of extracting useful information from existing raw data to create new predictive attributes is called:

options.A: Feature Estimating
options.B: Feature Engineering
options.C: Feature Enablement
options.D: Feature Evaluating

explanation: Feature engineering is the process of creating new predictive attributes from raw data — transforming, combining, or extracting information to improve model performance.

question: An engineer needs to restrict access to an Amazon S3 bucket whose objects contain PII data labeled per marketing campaign. Each ML engineer should only see data from their assigned campaign. Which approach provides the MOST scalable, fine-grained access control?

options.A: IAM policies on the AWS Glue Data Catalog restricting Amazon Athena access by campaign.
options.B: Amazon DynamoDB Streams with AWS Lambda updating S3 bucket policies dynamically.
options.C: AWS Lake Formation LF-Tags mapping engineers to their respective campaigns.
options.D: S3 bucket policies with conditions filtered by campaign prefix.

explanation: Lake Formation LF-Tags enable attribute-based access control, dynamically mapping users to data partitions (campaigns) without creating individual IAM policies per user-prefix combination.

question: A company is training a fraud model on a dataset that contains categorical features (occupation, country) and numerical features (transaction amount). Most ML algorithms require numeric input. Which solution transforms categorical data into numerical data with the LEAST effort?

options.A: Manually one-hot encode all categorical features in Jupyter notebooks.
options.B: Train the algorithm directly on raw categorical data without encoding.
options.C: Use Amazon SageMaker Data Wrangler to transform categorical data into numerical data.
options.D: Use Data Wrangler to transform numerical data into categorical data.

explanation: SageMaker Data Wrangler provides built-in categorical encoders (one-hot, ordinal, similarity) via a visual interface, converting categorical features to numeric with zero code.

question: A company uses Amazon SageMaker for experimentation, training, a central model registry, deployment, and monitoring. They need the central model registry to manage different versions of models with the LEAST operational overhead. Which approach should they use?

options.A: Create a separate Amazon Elastic Container Registry (Amazon ECR) repository for each model.
options.B: Use Amazon ECR with unique tags for each model version.
options.C: Use the SageMaker Model Registry with model groups to catalog the models.
options.D: Use the SageMaker Model Registry with only unique tags for each model version.

explanation: SageMaker Model Registry with model groups provides built-in versioning, approval workflows, lineage tracking, and metadata management — purpose-built for ML model lifecycle management.

question: A company is running consecutive training jobs with similar configurations and wants to MINIMIZE infrastructure startup times between jobs. Which SageMaker feature should they use?

options.A: Managed Spot Training
options.B: SageMaker managed warm pools
options.C: SageMaker Training Compiler
options.D: SageMaker distributed data parallelism (SMDDP) library

explanation: Managed warm pools keep training instances provisioned between consecutive jobs, cutting startup time from minutes to seconds for iterative model development.

question: An ML engineer has trained a neural network using stochastic gradient descent (SGD). During training, both the training and validation loss oscillate significantly — decreasing and then increasing repeatedly. What should the engineer do to stabilize training?

options.A: Introduce early stopping to halt training at the best validation loss.
options.B: Increase the size of the test set.
options.C: Increase the learning rate to converge faster.
options.D: Decrease the learning rate to allow more stable convergence.

explanation: Oscillating loss during SGD training means the learning rate is too large — gradient steps overshoot the loss minimum. Decreasing it allows smoother convergence.

question: An ML engineer observes that a model's training accuracy is 98% but validation accuracy is only 72%. Which technique should the engineer apply to address this issue?

options.A: Increase the number of training epochs.
options.B: Use a more complex model architecture.
options.C: Apply L1 or L2 regularization to the model.
options.D: Reduce the validation set size.

explanation: 98% training / 72% validation accuracy is classic overfitting. L1/L2 regularization constrains model weights, reducing complexity and improving generalization to unseen data.

question: A team wants automatic algorithm selection and hyperparameter optimization for a recommendation system. The training data is stored in Amazon S3. Which SageMaker feature provides this end-to-end AutoML capability?

options.A: Amazon SageMaker Autopilot
options.B: Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth
options.C: Amazon SageMaker Processing
options.D: Amazon SageMaker Neo

explanation: SageMaker Autopilot automates the complete ML workflow — data preprocessing, algorithm selection, hyperparameter tuning, and model ranking — for tabular datasets stored in S3.

question: An ML engineer must fine-tune a large language model (LLM) for text summarization using a low-code/no-code (LCNC) approach in Amazon SageMaker. Which approach should the engineer use?

options.A: Use SageMaker Studio to manually fine-tune on EC2 instances.
options.B: Use SageMaker Autopilot to fine-tune via a custom API endpoint.
options.C: Use SageMaker Autopilot directly on EC2 without JumpStart.
options.D: Use Amazon SageMaker Autopilot to fine-tune an LLM deployed by SageMaker JumpStart.

explanation: SageMaker JumpStart deploys pre-trained LLMs; Autopilot provides the low-code/no-code interface for fine-tuning — together they enable LLM customization without deep ML expertise.

question: An ML engineer must use a SageMaker built-in algorithm to detect fraudulent transactions. The features in the dataset are highly interdependent and have non-linear relationships. Which algorithm should the engineer choose?

options.A: Linear Learner
options.B: XGBoost
options.C: K-Means
options.D: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)

explanation: XGBoost excels at tabular data with non-linear feature interactions through gradient-boosted decision trees, making it the default choice for fraud detection on AWS.

question: An ML engineer uses Amazon SageMaker Canvas to build a custom model that produces continuous numeric predictions (e.g., sales forecasts over 10 years of historical data). Which evaluation metric is MOST appropriate for assessing this model?

options.A: Accuracy
options.B: InferenceLatency
options.C: AUC (Area Under the ROC Curve)
options.D: Root Mean Square Error (RMSE)

explanation: RMSE measures average prediction error magnitude for continuous numeric outputs, penalizing large errors more heavily — the standard regression metric for SageMaker Canvas models.

question: A regression model estimates used-phone resale prices (ranging from $50 to $1,500). Stakeholders want to know the average absolute prediction error expressed in USD. Which metric should the ML engineer report?

options.A: Accuracy
options.B: AUC
options.C: F1 Score
options.D: Mean Absolute Error (MAE)

explanation: MAE reports average absolute error in the target's units (USD), making it the most intuitive metric for stakeholders who want to understand prediction error in dollar terms.

question: A refinery is building a safety system to detect chemical leaks. Missing a real leak costs $120,000, while a false alarm costs only $300. When tuning the classifier's threshold, which metric should the ML engineer prioritize?

options.A: Low precision
options.B: High precision
options.C: Low recall
options.D: High recall

explanation: When missing a positive case (leak) costs far more than a false alarm, maximizing recall minimizes false negatives — the critical metric for safety-critical detection systems.

question: An ML engineer trains an XGBoost model in SageMaker with max_depth=10, eta=0.1, and subsample=1.0. The model achieves 0.99 AUC on training data but only 0.68 AUC on the held-out test set. Without changing the algorithm or features, what should the engineer do?

options.A: Increase the learning rate (eta).
options.B: Remove some features from the dataset.
options.C: Increase max_depth to 15.
options.D: Decrease max_depth to reduce model complexity.

explanation: With 0.99 train AUC vs 0.68 test AUC, the model is severely overfitting. Reducing max_depth limits tree complexity, forcing the model to learn generalizable patterns instead of memorizing.

question: A smart-farming system must detect ripe fruit in 4K drone images, providing bounding boxes and confidence scores of at least 0.85. Which SageMaker built-in algorithm should the ML engineer use?

options.A: Image Classification
options.B: XGBoost
options.C: Object Detection
options.D: k-Nearest Neighbors (k-NN)

explanation: SageMaker Object Detection returns bounding boxes with class labels and confidence scores for each detected object — designed for localizing and identifying objects within images.

question: In machine learning, the set of numeric input values used to represent a single observation is called:

options.A: A string vector
options.B: A feature vector
options.C: A function
options.D: An integer variable

explanation: A feature vector is the numeric representation of a single observation's attributes, used as input to ML algorithms for training and prediction.

question: Which statement BEST describes k-fold cross-validation?

options.A: The dataset is split once into a training set and a test set based on chronological order.
options.B: The dataset is split into k equally sized, randomly sampled, disjoint subsets. In each round, one subset is held out for testing while the remaining k-1 subsets are used for training.
options.C: The model is trained k times on the full dataset with different random seeds.
options.D: The dataset is duplicated k times, and each copy is trained with a different algorithm.

explanation: K-fold cross-validation rotates the held-out test fold across k splits, training on k-1 folds each time — providing a robust estimate of model performance that uses all data for both training and testing.

question: A company uses SageMaker Model Monitor on an XGBoost customer churn model. Several months after deployment, the F1 score decreases significantly. What is the MOST likely cause?

options.A: Concept drift occurred in the underlying customer data.
options.B: The model was not sufficiently complex at training time.
options.C: The baseline data used for Model Monitor had missing values.
options.D: Incorrect ground truth labels were used during baseline creation.

explanation: Concept drift means the statistical relationship between features and target has changed in the real world, causing model performance to degrade even though the model itself hasn't changed.

question: An ML engineer uses stochastic gradient descent (SGD) to train a model and wants to determine whether collecting more training data will improve performance or whether the model architecture itself needs revision. Which technique should the engineer use?

options.A: Learning Curves
options.B: Batch Normalization
options.C: Continuous Training
options.D: Data Augmentation

explanation: Learning curves plot training/validation performance vs. dataset size, revealing whether the model is data-limited (more data helps) or capacity-limited (architecture change needed).

question: A financial institution trains a binary classifier to detect fraud. What does the training phase learn?

options.A: A function that maximizes the loss over training examples.
options.B: An exponential feature set from the input data.
options.C: An exponential target variable.
options.D: A decision boundary between fraud and non-fraud classes.

explanation: A binary classifier learns a decision boundary — a function that separates the feature space into two regions (fraud vs. non-fraud), optimized by minimizing the training loss.

question: A team needs fine-grained control of ML workflows, including DAG visualization, experiment history, and model governance for auditing and compliance. Which combination of SageMaker features meets these requirements?

options.A: AWS CodePipeline + SageMaker Studio + ML Lineage Tracking
options.B: AWS CodePipeline + SageMaker Experiments
options.C: SageMaker Pipelines + SageMaker Studio + ML Lineage Tracking
options.D: SageMaker Pipelines + SageMaker Experiments only

explanation: SageMaker Pipelines provides native ML DAG orchestration; ML Lineage Tracking auto-captures data→model→endpoint relationships — together they enable full governance and reproducibility.

question: A company uses Amazon SageMaker and needs a manual approval workflow so that only approved models are deployed to production. How should the ML engineer implement this?

options.A: Use SageMaker Experiments to track approval status.
options.B: Use ML Lineage Tracking entities to mark models as approved.
options.C: Use SageMaker Model Monitor to manage model approval.
options.D: Use SageMaker Pipelines. When a model version is registered, use the AWS SDK to change the approval status to Approved.

explanation: Model Registry supports PendingApproval → Approved/Rejected status transitions via the AWS SDK (UpdateModelPackage API), enabling manual approval gates in ML deployment workflows.

question: A CI/CD pipeline must automatically retrain, test, and deploy a SageMaker model whenever new 10-GB datasets land in Amazon S3. The pipeline must also track model versions. Which solution meets these requirements?

options.A: AWS CodePipeline triggered by S3 events with AWS CodeBuild for training.
options.B: SageMaker Pipelines triggered by S3 events with SageMaker Model Registry for version tracking.
options.C: AWS Lambda triggered by Amazon EventBridge with AWS CloudTrail logs for auditing.
options.D: Manual notebook execution with CloudTrail for logging.

explanation: SageMaker Pipelines triggered by S3/EventBridge events automates the retrain→evaluate→register workflow. Model Registry tracks versions with metadata and approval status for auditing.

question: A model runs inference only once each night on approximately 3 MB of data. Each prediction takes less than 1 minute. The company wants to minimize cost. Which SageMaker inference option should the ML engineer use?

options.A: Multi-model serverless endpoint with caching enabled.
options.B: Asynchronous inference endpoint with InitialInstanceCount set to 0.
options.C: Real-time inference endpoint with auto scaling configured to scale to 0.
options.D: Serverless inference endpoint with MaxConcurrency set to 1.

explanation: Serverless inference auto-scales to zero between invocations, incurring cost only during active processing — the cheapest option for infrequent workloads with small payloads (≤6 MB).

question: A SageMaker endpoint must process payloads ranging from 100 MB to 300 MB, with each request taking up to 60 minutes to complete. Which inference option should the ML engineer choose?

options.A: Serverless inference
options.B: Asynchronous inference
options.C: Real-time inference
options.D: Batch transform

explanation: Asynchronous inference supports payloads up to 1 GB and processing times up to 60 minutes, queuing requests in S3 — real-time endpoints cap at 6 MB payload and 60-second timeout.

question: A model trained in SageMaker must be highly available with minimum latency. Requests range from 1 KB to 3 MB with unpredictable traffic bursts. Inference capacity must adapt proportionally to demand. Which deployment option should the ML engineer use?

options.A: Real-time inference endpoint with auto scaling.
options.B: Amazon ECS cluster with scheduled scaling.
options.C: Multi-model endpoint with a fixed number of instances.
options.D: Multiple separate endpoints behind an Application Load Balancer.

explanation: Real-time endpoints with target-tracking auto scaling provide persistent, low-latency inference that automatically adjusts capacity to match unpredictable traffic patterns.

question: A deployed real-time SageMaker endpoint has higher-than-expected latency on inference requests. CloudWatch metrics show high CPU utilization. What is the MOST effective immediate action?

options.A: Increase the request batch size to process more requests per call.
options.B: Enable multi-model endpoints to share resources.
options.C: Increase the instance size or the number of instances behind the endpoint.
options.D: Switch to asynchronous inference to handle requests in a queue.

explanation: High CPU utilization on a real-time endpoint indicates under-provisioning. Scaling up (larger instances) or scaling out (more instances) directly addresses the compute bottleneck.

question: A video platform wants to evaluate a new model version using 24 hours of live production traffic. The platform must store the new model's inferences offline for analysis but must NOT affect production responses or latency. Which SageMaker feature should the ML engineer use?

options.A: SageMaker Debugger with a custom rule.
options.B: Blue/green deployment with all-at-once traffic shifting.
options.C: Blue/green deployment with canary traffic shifting at 10% for 24 hours.
options.D: SageMaker shadow testing with a shadow variant.

explanation: Shadow testing mirrors 100% of production traffic to a shadow variant, capturing its predictions for offline analysis without affecting the production model's responses or latency.

question: An ML engineer wants to orchestrate an end-to-end workflow that includes data processing, model training, hyperparameter tuning, and deployment — all within Amazon SageMaker. Which service should the engineer use?

options.A: Implement the entire workflow with SageMaker Pipelines.
options.B: Use AWS Step Functions only.
options.C: Use Amazon Managed Workflows for Apache Airflow (MWAA).
options.D: Chain AWS Lambda functions manually.

explanation: SageMaker Pipelines is the native ML orchestration service, providing DAG-based workflow management with built-in steps for processing, training, tuning, evaluation, and deployment.

question: A long-running deep learning training job can tolerate interruptions. The ML engineer wants to minimize cost. Which SageMaker feature should the engineer use?

options.A: Managed Spot Training
options.B: Managed Warm Pools
options.C: SageMaker Training Compiler
options.D: SageMaker distributed data parallelism (SMDDP) library

explanation: Managed Spot Training uses spare EC2 capacity at up to 90% discount. With checkpointing enabled, interrupted training resumes automatically — ideal for long-running, cost-sensitive jobs.

question: A media company has 200+ ML models across 12 AWS accounts, each stored in account-local Amazon ECR repositories. The company needs a single cross-account central catalog with model versioning, approval tracking, lineage, and audit capabilities — WITHOUT duplicating container images. Which solution meets these requirements?

options.A: Enable Amazon ECR cross-account replication for every repository.
options.B: Create a central ECR repository in a governance account with replication from all other accounts.
options.C: Create a SageMaker Model Registry in a governance account with model packages referencing source ECR URIs and cross-account resource policies.
options.D: Use the AWS Glue Data Catalog to crawl and catalog ECR repositories across accounts.

explanation: Model Registry model packages store ECR image URIs (not copies), enabling cross-account sharing via resource policies without duplicating container images across accounts.

question: A platform has 1,200+ model versions organized in 95 model groups across three business domains. The ML team wants to organize model groups by domain without altering any existing artifacts, versions, approval statuses, or group memberships. Which solution meets this requirement?

options.A: Tag every model package with its business domain.
options.B: Create new model groups per domain and migrate all model versions.
options.C: Use ML Lineage Tracking to automatically tag models by domain.
options.D: Use SageMaker Model Registry collections to organize existing model groups into domain-based collections.

explanation: Model Registry collections group model groups hierarchically without modifying underlying artifacts, versions, or approval statuses — purely an organizational abstraction layer.

question: An ML team configures auto scaling for a SageMaker real-time inference endpoint. Which CloudWatch metric is the built-in default for target tracking scaling policies on SageMaker endpoints?

options.A: Step scaling on custom CPUUtilization metric.
options.B: Simple scaling on an Invocations CloudWatch alarm.
options.C: Target tracking on ApproximateBacklogSizePerInstance.
options.D: Target tracking on SageMakerVariantInvocationsPerInstance.

explanation: SageMakerVariantInvocationsPerInstance is the predefined CloudWatch metric for target-tracking auto scaling on SageMaker real-time endpoints, measuring request load per instance.

question: A company needs to orchestrate an ML workflow that includes a SageMaker training job, an AWS Lambda data-validation step, and conditional retries on failure. Which AWS service provides native retry/error-handling and visual workflow capabilities for this use case?

options.A: AWS Step Functions with Retry and Catch blocks in the ASL definition.
options.B: AWS CodePipeline only.
options.C: Amazon EventBridge rules.
options.D: AWS CloudFormation custom resources.
